text.DRV: 4:16 saying: What shall we do to these men? for a notorious sign indeed hath been done by them, to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem: it is manifest, and we cannot deny it.
text.NABRE: 4:16 “What are we to do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem knows that a remarkable sign was done through them, and we cannot deny it.

text.NRSV: “But to keep it from spreading further among the people, let us warn them to speak no more to anyone in this name.”
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, lest it further spread abroad among the people, let us charge them with threats, to be no more speaking upon this name, to any soul of man.
text.KJV: 4:17 But that it spread no farther among the people, let vs straitly threaten them, that they speake henceforth to no man in this Name.
text.DRV: 4:17 But that it be no further spread abroad among the people, let us threaten them, that they speak no more in this name to any man.
text.NABRE: 4:17 But so that it may not be spread any further among the people, let us give them a stern warning never again to speak to anyone in this name.”

text.NRSV: So they called them and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, calling them, they gave them the sweeping charge,—not to be sounding aloud, nor even to be teaching, upon the name of Jesus.
text.KJV: 4:18 And they called them, and commanded them, not to speake at all, nor teach in the Name of Iesus.
text.DRV: 4:18 And calling them, they charged them that they should not speak at all, nor teach in the name of Jesus.
text.NABRE: 4:18 So they called them back and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.

text.NRSV: But Peter and John answered them, “Whether it is right in God’s sight to listen to you rather than to God, you must judge;
text.Rotherham: But, Peter and John, answering, said unto them—Whether it be right in presence of God, unto you, to be hearkening, rather than unto God, judge!
text.KJV: 4:19 But Peter and Iohn answered, and said vnto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God, to hearken vnto you more then vnto God, iudge ye.
text.DRV: 4:19 But Peter and John answering, said to them: If it be just in the sight of God, to hear you rather than God, judge ye.
text.NABRE: 4:19 Peter and John, however, said to them in reply, “Whether it is right in the sight of God for us to obey you rather than God, you be the judges.

text.NRSV: for we cannot keep from speaking about what we have seen and heard.”
text.Rotherham: For, we, cannot but be speaking the things which we have seen and heard.
text.KJV: 4:20 For wee cannot but speake the things which we haue seene and heard.
text.DRV: 4:20 For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.
text.NABRE: 4:20 It is impossible for us not to speak about what we have seen and heard.”

text.NRSV: After threatening them further, they let them go, finding no way to punish them because of the people, for all of them praised God for what had happened.
text.Rotherham: They, however, further charging them with threats, let them go, finding nothing as to how they might chastise them, on account of the people; because, all, were glorifying God for that which had come to pass;—
text.KJV: 4:21 So when they had further threatned them, they let them goe, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.
text.DRV: 4:21 But they threatening, dismissed them: not finding how they might punish them, for the people, because all glorified that which had been done, in that which was chanced.
text.NABRE: 4:21 After threatening them further, they released them, finding no way to punish them, on account of the people who were all praising God for what had happened.

text.NRSV: For the man on whom this sign of healing had been performed was more than forty years old.
text.Rotherham: For, more than forty years old, was the man, upon whom had taken place, this healing sign.
text.KJV: 4:22 For the man was aboue fourtie yeeres olde, on whome this miracle of healing was shewed.
text.DRV: 4:22 For the man was more than forty years old in whom that sign of health had been wrought.
text.NABRE: 4:22 For the man on whom this sign of healing had been done was over forty years old.

text.NRSV: After they were released, they went to their own people and reported what the chief priests and the elders had said to them.
text.Rotherham: But, when they had been let go, they came unto their own [friends], and told as many things as, unto them, the High-priests and Elders, had said.
text.KJV: 4:23 ¶ And being let goe, they went to their owne company, and reported all that the chiefe Priests and Elders had said vnto them.
text.DRV: 4:23 And being dismissed they came to theirs, and shewed all that the chief priests and Ancients had said to them.
text.NABRE: 4:23 Prayer of the Community. After their release they went back to their own people and reported what the chief priests and elders had told them.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they raised their voices together to God and said, “Sovereign Lord, who made the heaven and the earth, the sea, and everything in them,
text.Rotherham: And, they, having heard, with one accord, uplifted voice unto God, and said—O Sovereign! Thou, art he that made the heaven, and the earth, and the sea, and all things that are herein:
text.KJV: 4:24 And when they heard that, they lift vp their voyce to God with one accord, & said, Lord, thou art God which hast made heauen and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is,
text.DRV: 4:24 Who having heard it, with one accord lifted up their voice to God, and said: Lord, thou that didst make Heaven & earth, the sea, and all things that are in them,
text.NABRE: 4:24 And when they heard it, they raised their voices to God with one accord and said, “Sovereign Lord, maker of heaven and earth and the sea and all that is in them,

text.NRSV: it is you who said by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: ‘Why did the Gentiles rage, and the peoples imagine vain things?
text.Rotherham: Who, by our father, through means of the Holy Spirit, even by the mouth of David thy servant, said—Unto what end did nations rage, and, peoples, busy themselves with empty things?
text.KJV: 4:25 Who by the mouth of thy seruant Dauid hast saide, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vaine things?
text.DRV: 4:25 who in the Holy Ghost by the mouth of our Father David thy servant hast said: Why did the Gentiles rage, and the people meditate vain things:
text.NABRE: 4:25 you said by the holy Spirit through the mouth of our father David, your servant: ‘Why did the Gentiles rage and the peoples entertain folly?

text.NRSV: The kings of the earth took their stand, and the rulers have gathered together against the Lord and against his Messiah.’
text.Rotherham: The kings of the earth stationed themselves, and, the rulers, were gathered together, with one intent, against the Lord, and against his Christ.
text.KJV: 4:26 The Kings of the earth stood vp, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, & against his Christ.
text.DRV: 4:26 the kings of the earth stand up, and the Princes assemble together against our Lord, and against his Christ?
text.NABRE: 4:26 The kings of the earth took their stand and the princes gathered together against the Lord and against his anointed.’

text.NRSV: For in this city, in fact, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel, gathered together against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed,
text.Rotherham: For they were gathered together, of a truth, in this city, against thy holy servant Jesus, whom thou hadst anointed,—both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with them of the nations, and peoples of Israel;—
text.KJV: 4:27 For of a trueth against thy holy child Iesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel were gathered together,
text.DRV: 4:27 For there assembled indeed in this city against the holy child Jesus whom thou hast anointed, Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel;
text.NABRE: 4:27 Indeed they gathered in this city against your holy servant Jesus whom you anointed, Herod and Pontius Pilate, together with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel,

text.NRSV: to do whatever your hand and your plan had predestined to take place.
text.Rotherham: To do whatsoever, thy hand and thy counsel, marked out beforehand to come to pass.
text.KJV: 4:28 For to doe whatsoeuer thy hand and thy counsell determined before to be done.
text.DRV: 4:28 to do what thy hand and thy counsel decreed to be done.
text.NABRE: 4:28 to do what your hand and [your] will had long ago planned to take place.

text.NRSV: And now, Lord, look at their threats, and grant to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,
text.Rotherham: As to the present things, then, O Lord,—look upon their threats, and grant unto thy servants, with all freedom of utterance, to be speaking thy word,
text.KJV: 4:29 And now Lord, behold their threatnings, and graunt vnto thy seruants, that with all boldnesse they may speake thy word,
text.DRV: 4:29 And now Lord look upon their threatenings, and give unto thy servants with all confidence to speak thy word,
text.NABRE: 4:29 And now, Lord, take note of their threats, and enable your servants to speak your word with all boldness,

text.NRSV: while you stretch out your hand to heal, and signs and wonders are performed through the name of your holy servant Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: By stretching forth thy hand for healing, and by the coming to pass of both signs and wonders, through the name of thy holy servant Jesus.
text.KJV: 4:30 By stretching foorth thine hand to heale: and that signes and wonders may be done by the Name of thy holy child Iesus.
text.DRV: 4:30 in that, that thou extend thy hand to cures & signs and wonders to be done by the name of thy holy son Jesus.
text.NABRE: 4:30 as you stretch forth [your] hand to heal, and signs and wonders are done through the name of your holy servant Jesus.”

text.NRSV: When they had prayed, the place in which they were assembled was shaken; and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God with boldness.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had made supplication, the place was shaken in which they were gathered together, and they were filled, one and all, with the Holy Spirit, and began speaking the word of God with freedom of utterance.
text.KJV: 4:31 ¶ And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together, and they were all filled with the holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldnesse.
text.DRV: 4:31 And when they had prayed, the place was moved wherein they were gathered: and they were all replenished with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with confidence.
text.NABRE: 4:31 As they prayed, the place where they were gathered shook, and they were all filled with the holy Spirit and continued to speak the word of God with boldness. Life in the Christian Community.

text.NRSV: Now the whole group of those who believed were of one heart and soul, and no one claimed private ownership of any possessions, but everything they owned was held in common.
text.Rotherham: And, the throng of them that believed, had one heart and soul, and not so much as one was saying that, aught of his goods, was, his own, but they had all things common.
text.KJV: 4:32 And the multitude of them that beleeued, were of one heart, and of one soule: Neither said any of them, that ought of the things which he possessed, was his owne, but they had all things common.
text.DRV: 4:32 And the multitude of believers had one heart and one soul: neither did any one say that ought was his own of those things which he possessed, but all things were common unto them.
text.NABRE: 4:32 The community of believers was of one heart and mind, and no one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but they had everything in common.

text.NRSV: With great power the apostles gave their testimony to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.
text.Rotherham: And, with great power, were the apostles giving forth their witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus; great favour also was upon them all.
text.KJV: 4:33 And with great power gaue the Apostles witnesse of the resurrection of the Lord Iesus, and great grace was vpon them all.
text.DRV: 4:33 And with great power did the Apostles give testimony of the resurrection of Jesus Christ our Lord: and great grace was in all them.
text.NABRE: 4:33 With great power the apostles bore witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great favor was accorded them all.

text.NRSV: There was not a needy person among them, for as many as owned lands or houses sold them and brought the proceeds of what was sold.
text.Rotherham: For there was not so much as anyone, lacking, among them; for, as many as were possessors of lands or houses, selling them, were bringing the prices of the things that were being sold,
text.KJV: 4:34 Neither was there any among them that lacked: For as many as were possessors of lands, or houses, sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were solde,
text.DRV: 4:34 For neither was there any one needy among them. For as many as were owners of lands or houses, sold and brought the prices of those things which they sold,
text.NABRE: 4:34 There was no needy person among them, for those who owned property or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds of the sale,

text.NRSV: They laid it at the apostles’ feet, and it was distributed to each as any had need.
text.Rotherham: And laying them at the feet of the Apostles, while on the other hand they were distributing unto each one, in so far as any one had, need.
text.KJV: 4:35 And laide them downe at the Apostles feete: And distribution was made vnto euery man according as hee had neede.
text.DRV: 4:35 and laid it before the feet of the Apostles. And to every one was divided according as every one had need.
text.NABRE: 4:35 and put them at the feet of the apostles, and they were distributed to each according to need.

text.NRSV: There was a Levite, a native of Cyprus, Joseph, to whom the apostles gave the name Barnabas (which means “son of encouragement”).
text.Rotherham: And, Joseph, who had been surnamed Barnabas by the Apostles, which is to be translated Son of Exhortation,—a Levite, a Cyprian by nation,
text.KJV: 4:36 And Ioses, who by the Apostles was surnamed Barnabas (which is, being interpreted, The sonne of consolation) a Leuite, and of the Countrey of Cyprus,
text.DRV: 4:36 And Joseph who was surnamed of the Apostles Barnabas (which is by interpretation, the son of consolation) a Levite, a Cyprian born,
text.NABRE: 4:36 Thus Joseph, also named by the apostles Barnabas (which is translated “son of encouragement”), a Levite, a Cypriot by birth,

text.NRSV: He sold a field that belonged to him, then brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.Rotherham: Having a field, sold it, and brought the money and laid it at the feet of the Apostles.
text.KJV: 4:37 Hauing land, sold it, and brought the money, & laid it at the Apostles feet.
text.DRV: 4:37 whereas he had a piece of land, sold it, and brought the price, and laid it before the feet of the Apostles.
text.NABRE: 4:37 sold a piece of property that he owned, then brought the money and put it at the feet of the apostles.

text.NRSV: But a man named Ananias, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property;
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man, Ananias by name, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
text.KJV: 5:1 But a certaine man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, solde a possession,
text.DRV: 5:1 But a certain man named Ananias, with Saphira his wife sold a piece of land,
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 - Ananias and Sapphira. A man named Ananias, however, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.

text.NRSV: with his wife’s knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds, and brought only a part and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.Rotherham: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it; and, bringing a certain part, at the feet of the Apostles, he laid it.
text.KJV: 5:2 And kept backe part of the price, his wife also being priuy to it, and brought a certaine part, and layd it at the Apostles feete.
text.DRV: 5:2 and defrauded of the price of the land, his wife being privy thereto: and bringing a certain portion, laid it at the feet of the Apostles.
text.NABRE: 5:2 He retained for himself, with his wife’s knowledge, some of the purchase price, took the remainder, and put it at the feet of the apostles.

text.NRSV: “Ananias,” Peter said, “why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back part of the proceeds of the land?
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—Ananias! wherefore did Satan fill thy heart, that thou shouldst deal falsely with the Holy Spirit, and keep back part of the price of the field?
text.KJV: 5:3 But Peter said, Ananias, Why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the holy Ghost, and to keepe backe part of the price of the land?
text.DRV: 5:3 And Peter said: Ananias, why hath Satan tempted thy heart, that thou shouldest lie to the Holy Ghost, and defraud of the price of the land?
text.NABRE: 5:3 But Peter said, “Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart so that you lied to the holy Spirit and retained part of the price of the land?

text.NRSV: While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, were not the proceeds at your disposal? How is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You did not lie to us but to God!”
text.Rotherham: While it remained, was it not, as thine own, it remained? and, when sold, was it not, in thine own authority, that it still continued? Why was it that thou didst contrive in thy heart this deed? Thou hast not dealt falsely with men but, with God.
text.KJV: 5:4 Whiles it remained, was it not thine owne? and after it was sold, was it not in thine owne power? why hast thou conceiued this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied vnto men, but vnto God.
text.DRV: 5:4 Remaining, did it not remain to thee: and being sold, was it not in thy power? Why hast thou put this thing in thy heart? Thou hast not lied to men, but to God.
text.NABRE: 5:4 While it remained unsold, did it not remain yours? And when it was sold, was it not still under your control? Why did you contrive this deed? You have lied not to human beings, but to God.”

text.NRSV: Now when Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard of it.
text.Rotherham: And, as Ananias heard these words, he fell, and expired. And there came great fear upon all that heard;
text.KJV: 5:5 And Ananias hearing these words, fell downe, and gaue vp the ghost: and great feare came on all them that heard these things.
text.DRV: 5:5 And Ananias hearing these words, fell down, and gave up the ghost. And there came great fear upon all that heard it.
text.NABRE: 5:5 When Ananias heard these words, he fell down and breathed his last, and great fear came upon all who heard of it.

text.NRSV: The young men came and wrapped up his body, then carried him out and buried him.
text.Rotherham: But the young men, rising up, wrapped him about, and, bearing him forth, buried him.
text.KJV: 5:6 And the yong men arose, wound him vp, and caried him out, and buried him.
text.DRV: 5:6 And young men rising up, removed him, & bearing him forth buried him.
text.NABRE: 5:6 The young men came and wrapped him up, then carried him out and buried him.

text.NRSV: After an interval of about three hours his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after about three hours interval, that his wife, not knowing what had happened, came in.
text.KJV: 5:7 And it was about the space of three houres after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
text.DRV: 5:7 And it was the space as it were of three hours, and his wife, not knowing what was chanced, came in.
text.NABRE: 5:7 After an interval of about three hours, his wife came in, unaware of what had happened.

text.NRSV: Peter said to her, “Tell me whether you and your husband sold the land for such and such a price.” And she said, “Yes, that was the price.”
text.Rotherham: And Peter began to say unto her—Tell me! was it, for so much, ye gave up the field? And she said—Yea! for so much.
text.KJV: 5:8 And Peter answered vnto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much. And she saide, Yea, for so much.
text.DRV: 5:8 And Peter answered her: Tell me woman, whether did you sell the land for so much? But she said: Yea, for so much.
text.NABRE: 5:8 Peter said to her, “Tell me, did you sell the land for this amount?” She answered, “Yes, for that amount.”

text.NRSV: Then Peter said to her, “How is it that you have agreed together to put the Spirit of the Lord to the test? Look, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, [said] unto her—Why was it agreed by you to put to the proof the Spirit of the Lord? Lo! the feet of them that have buried thy husband, are at the door, and they shall bear thee forth.
text.KJV: 5:9 Then Peter saide vnto her, How is it that ye haue agreed together, to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feete of them which haue buried thy husband, are at the doore, and shall cary thee out.
text.DRV: 5:9 And Peter unto her: Why have you agreed together to tempt the Spirit of our Lord? Behold, their feet that have buried thy husband, at the door, and they shall bear thee forth.
text.NABRE: 5:9 Then Peter said to her, “Why did you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord? Listen, the footsteps of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”

text.NRSV: Immediately she fell down at his feet and died. When the young men came in they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text.Rotherham: And she fell instantly at his feet, and expired. And the young men, coming in, found her dead; and, bearing her forth, they buried her with her husband.
text.KJV: 5:10 Then fell she downe straightway at his feete, and yeelded vp the ghost: And the yong men came in, and found her dead, and carying her forth, buried her by her husband.
text.DRV: 5:10 Forthwith she fell before his feet, and gave up the ghost. And the young men going in, found her dead: and carried her forth, & buried her by her husband.
text.NABRE: 5:10 At once, she fell down at his feet and breathed her last. When the young men entered they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.

text.NRSV: And great fear seized the whole church and all who heard of these things.
text.Rotherham: And there came great fear upon the whole assembly, and upon all them that heard these things.
text.KJV: 5:11 And great feare came vpon all the Church, and vpon as many as heard these things.
text.DRV: 5:11 And there fell great fear in the whole Church, and upon all that heard these things.
text.NABRE: 5:11 And great fear came upon the whole church and upon all who heard of these things. Signs and Wonders of the Apostles.

text.NRSV: Now many signs and wonders were done among the people through the apostles. And they were all together in Solomon’s Portico.
text.Rotherham: And, through the hands of the Apostles, were coming to pass many signs and wonders among the people; and they were all with one accord in the portico of Solomon;
text.KJV: 5:12 ¶ And by the hands of the Apostles, were many signes and wonders wrought among the people. (And they were all with one accord in Solomons porch.
text.DRV: 5:12 And by the hands of the Apostles were many signs and wonders done among the people. And they were all with one accord in Salomon's porch.
text.NABRE: 5:12 Many signs and wonders were done among the people at the hands of the apostles. They were all together in Solomon’s portico.

text.NRSV: None of the rest dared to join them, but the people held them in high esteem.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, of the rest, no one, durst join himself unto them,—nevertheless the people continued to magnify them;
text.KJV: 5:13 And of the rest durst no man ioyne himselfe to them: But the people magnified them.
text.DRV: 5:13 But of the rest none durst join themselves unto them: but the people magnified them.
text.NABRE: 5:13 None of the others dared to join them, but the people esteemed them.

text.NRSV: Yet more than ever believers were added to the Lord, great numbers of both men and women,
text.Rotherham: And, the more, were being added, when they believed in the Lord, throngs both of men and women;—
text.KJV: 5:14 And beleeuers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
text.DRV: 5:14 And the multitude of men and women that believed in our Lord, was more increased:
text.NABRE: 5:14 Yet more than ever, believers in the Lord, great numbers of men and women, were added to them.

text.NRSV: so that they even carried out the sick into the streets, and laid them on cots and mats, in order that Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he came by.
text.Rotherham: So that, even into the broad-ways, were they bringing forth the sick, and laying them on small couches and beds, in order that, if Peter were coming along, even perchance his shadow, might overshadow some one of them.
text.KJV: 5:15 Insomuch þ they brought foorth the sicke into the streetes, and layed them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by, might ouershadow some of them.
text.DRV: 5:15 so that they did bring forth the sick into the streets, & laid them in beds and couches, that when Peter came, his shadow at the least might overshadow any of them, and they all might be delivered from their infirmities.
text.NABRE: 5:15 Thus they even carried the sick out into the streets and laid them on cots and mats so that when Peter came by, at least his shadow might fall on one or another of them.

text.NRSV: A great number of people would also gather from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean spirits, and they were all cured.
text.Rotherham: Moreover even the throng of the cities all round Jerusalem was coming together, bearing sick folk, and such as were harassed by impure spirits,—who, indeed, were being cured, one and all.
text.KJV: 5:16 There came also a multitude out of the cities round about vnto Hierusalem, bringing sicke folkes, and them which were vexed with vncleane spirits: and they were healed euery one.
text.DRV: 5:16 And there ran together unto Jerusalem the multitude also of the cities adjoining, bringing sick persons and such as were vexed of unclean spirits; who were all cured.
text.NABRE: 5:16 A large number of people from the towns in the vicinity of Jerusalem also gathered, bringing the sick and those disturbed by unclean spirits, and they were all cured. Trial Before the Sanhedrin.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest took action; he and all who were with him (that is, the sect of the Sadducees), being filled with jealousy,
text.Rotherham: But the High-priest, arising, and all who were with him,—being the sect of the Sadducees,—were filled with jealousy,
text.KJV: 5:17 ¶ Then the high Priest rose vp, and al they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadduces) and were filled with indignation,
text.DRV: 5:17 And the high Priest rising up, and all that were with him, which is the heresy of the Sadducees, were replenished with zeal:
text.NABRE: 5:17 Then the high priest rose up and all his companions, that is, the party of the Sadducees, and, filled with jealousy,

text.NRSV: arrested the apostles and put them in the public prison.
text.Rotherham: And thrust their hands upon the Apostles, and put them in a public ward.
text.KJV: 5:18 And laid their hands on the Apostles, & put them in the common prison.
text.DRV: 5:18 laid hands upon the Apostles, and put them in the common prison.
text.NABRE: 5:18 laid hands upon the apostles and put them in the public jail.

text.NRSV: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors, brought them out, and said,
text.Rotherham: But, a messenger of the Lord, by night, opened the doors of the prison; and, leading them out, said—
text.KJV: 5:19 But the Angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doores, and brought them foorth, and said,
text.DRV: 5:19 But an Angel of our Lord by night opening the gates of the prison, & leading them forth, said:
text.NABRE: 5:19 But during the night, the angel of the Lord opened the doors of the prison, led them out, and said,

text.NRSV: “Go, stand in the temple and tell the people the whole message about this life.”
text.Rotherham: Be going your way, and, taking your stand, be speaking, in the temple, unto the people, all the declarations of this Life.
text.KJV: 5:20 Goe, stand and speake in the Temple to the people all the words of this life.
text.DRV: 5:20 Go; and standing speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
text.NABRE: 5:20 “Go and take your place in the temple area, and tell the people everything about this life.”

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they entered the temple at daybreak and went on with their teaching. When the high priest and those with him arrived, they called together the council and the whole body of the elders of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard this, they entered, under the dawn, into the temple, and began teaching. And the High-priest and they who were with him, arriving, called together the high-council, and all the senate of the sons of Israel; and sent off unto the prison, to have them brought.
text.KJV: 5:21 And when they heard that, they entred into the Temple early in the morning, & taught: but the high Priest came, and they that were with him, and called the Councill together, and all the Senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to haue them brought.
text.DRV: 5:21 Who having heard this, early in the morning entered into the temple, and taught. And the high priest coming, and they that were with him, called together the Council & all the ancients of the children of Israel: and they sent to the prison that they might be brought.
text.NABRE: 5:21 When they heard this, they went to the temple early in the morning and taught. When the high priest and his companions arrived, they convened the Sanhedrin, the full senate of the Israelites, and sent to the jail to have them brought in.

text.NRSV: But when the temple police arrived, they did not find them in the prison; so they returned and reported,
text.Rotherham: And, the officers who came to the place, found them not in the prison; and, returning, brought tidings,
text.KJV: 5:22 But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
text.DRV: 5:22 But when the ministers were come, and opening the prison, found them not; returning they told, saying:
text.NABRE: 5:22 But the court officers who went did not find them in the prison, so they came back and reported,

text.NRSV: “We found the prison securely locked and the guards standing at the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
text.Rotherham: Saying—The prison, found we locked with all safety, and the keepers standing at the doors; but, when we had opened the prison, inside, found we, no one.
text.KJV: 5:23 Saying, The prison truely found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doores, but when we had opened, we found no man within.
text.DRV: 5:23 The prison truly we found shut with all diligence, and the keepers standing before the gates: but opening it, we found no man within.
text.NABRE: 5:23 “We found the jail securely locked and the guards stationed outside the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”

text.NRSV: Now when the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were perplexed about them, wondering what might be going on.
text.Rotherham: Now, when both the captain of the temple and the High-priests heard these words, they were utterly at a loss concerning them—what perhaps this might come to.
text.KJV: 5:24 Now when the high Priest, and the captaine of the Temple, and the chiefe Priests heard these things, they doubted of them wherunto this would grow.
text.DRV: 5:24 And as soon as the Magistrates of the temple and the chief Priests heard these words, they were in doubt of them, what would befall.
text.NABRE: 5:24 When they heard this report, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were at a loss about them, as to what this would come to.

text.NRSV: Then someone arrived and announced, “Look, the men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people!”
text.Rotherham: But some one, entering, brought tidings unto them—Lo! the men whom ye put in the prison, are in the temple, standing, and teaching the people!
text.KJV: 5:25 Then came one, and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison, are standing in the Temple, and teaching the people.
text.DRV: 5:25 And there came a certain man and told them: That the men, lo, which you did put in prison, are in the temple standing, and teaching the people.
text.NABRE: 5:25 Then someone came in and reported to them, “The men whom you put in prison are in the temple area and are teaching the people.”

text.NRSV: Then the captain went with the temple police and brought them, but without violence, for they were afraid of being stoned by the people.
text.Rotherham: Then, the captain with the officers, departing, brought them—not with violence,—for they were afraid of the people, lest they should be stoned;
text.KJV: 5:26 Then went the captaine with the officers, and brought them without violence: (For they feared the people, lest they should haue bene stoned.)
text.DRV: 5:26 Then went the Magistrate with the ministers, & brought them without force, for they feared the people, lest they should be stoned.
text.NABRE: 5:26 Then the captain and the court officers went and brought them in, but without force, because they were afraid of being stoned by the people.

text.NRSV: When they had brought them, they had them stand before the council. The high priest questioned them,
text.Rotherham: But, leading them in, set them in the high- council. And the High-priest questioned them,
text.KJV: 5:27 And when they had brought them, they set them before the Councill, and the high Priest asked them,
text.DRV: 5:27 And when they had brought them, they set them in the Council. And the high Priest asked them,
text.NABRE: 5:27 When they had brought them in and made them stand before the Sanhedrin, the high priest questioned them,

text.NRSV: saying, “We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and you are determined to bring this man’s blood on us.”
text.Rotherham: Saying—Strictly, did we charge you not to be teaching upon this name; and lo! ye have filled Jerusalem with your teaching, and are minded to bring down upon us, the blood of this man.
text.KJV: 5:28 Saying, Did not wee straitly command you, that you should not teach in this Name? And behold, yee haue filled Hierusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this mans blood vpon vs.
text.DRV: 5:28 saying: Commanding we commanded you that you should not teach in this name: and behold you have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and you will bring upon us the blood of this man.
text.NABRE: 5:28 “We gave you strict orders [did we not?] to stop teaching in that name. Yet you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and want to bring this man’s blood upon us.”

text.NRSV: But Peter and the apostles answered, “We must obey God rather than any human authority.
text.Rotherham: But Peter and the Apostles, answering, said—It is needful to be yielding obedience unto God rather than unto men:—
text.KJV: 5:29 ¶ Then Peter, and the other Apostles answered, and saide, Wee ought to obey God rather then men.
text.DRV: 5:29 But Peter answering and the Apostles, said: God must be obeyed, rather than men.
text.NABRE: 5:29 But Peter and the apostles said in reply, “We must obey God rather than men.

text.NRSV: The God of our ancestors raised up Jesus, whom you had killed by hanging him on a tree.
text.Rotherham: The God of our fathers, hath raised up Jesus,—whom, ye, got into your hands, suspending him upon a tree:—
text.KJV: 5:30 The God of our fathers raised vp Iesus, whom yee slew and hanged on a tree.
text.DRV: 5:30 The God of our Fathers hath raised up Jesus, whom you did kill, hanging him upon a tree.
text.NABRE: 5:30 The God of our ancestors raised Jesus, though you had him killed by hanging him on a tree.

text.NRSV: God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior that he might give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: Him, as Princely-leader and Saviour, hath God exalted unto his right hand,—to give repentance unto Israel, and remission of sins.
text.KJV: 5:31 Him hath God exalted with his right hand to bee a Prince and a Sauiour, for to giue repentance to Israel, and forgiuenesse of sinnes.
text.DRV: 5:31 This Prince and Saviour God hath exalted with his right hand, to give repentance to Israel, and remission of sins.
text.NABRE: 5:31 God exalted him at his right hand as leader and savior to grant Israel repentance and forgiveness of sins.

text.NRSV: And we are witnesses to these things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him.”
text.Rotherham: And, we, are witnesses of these things,—also the Holy Spirit, which God hath given unto them who are yielding obedience unto him.
text.KJV: 5:32 And we are his witnesses of these things, and so is also the holy Ghost, whom God hath giuen to them that obey him.
text.DRV: 5:32 And we are witnesses of these words, and the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to all that obey him.
text.NABRE: 5:32 We are witnesses of these things, as is the holy Spirit that God has given to those who obey him.”

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.
text.Rotherham: And, they, when they heard this, were cut to the heart, and were making up their minds to slay them.
text.KJV: 5:33 ¶ When they heard that, they were cut to the heart, and tooke counsell to slay them.
text.DRV: 5:33 When they had heard these things, it cut them to the heart, and they consulted to kill them.
text.NABRE: 5:33 When they heard this, they became infuriated and wanted to put them to death.

text.NRSV: But a Pharisee in the council named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up and ordered the men to be put outside for a short time.
text.Rotherham: But a certain man, rising up in the high-council, by name Gamaliel, a law-teacher honoured by all the people, gave orders to put the men, outside, for a little,—
text.KJV: 5:34 Then stood there vp one in the Councill, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctour of Law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the Apostles forth a litle space,
text.DRV: 5:34 But one in the Council rising up, a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a Doctor of law honourable to all the people, commanded the men to be put forth a while.
text.NABRE: 5:34 But a Pharisee in the Sanhedrin named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up, ordered the men to be put outside for a short time,

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you propose to do to these men.
text.Rotherham: And said unto them—Men of Israel! be taking heed unto yourselves, what ye are about to inflict upon these men.
text.KJV: 5:35 And said vnto them, Yee men of Israel, take heed to your selues, what ye intend to doe as touching these men.
text.DRV: 5:35 and he said to them: Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves touching these men what you mean to do.
text.NABRE: 5:35 and said to them, “Fellow Israelites, be careful what you are about to do to these men.

text.NRSV: For some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a number of men, about four hundred, joined him; he was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and came to nothing.
text.Rotherham: For, before these days, there rose up one Theudas, affirming, himself, to be somebody,—unto whom was inclined a number of men, about four hundred; who was slain, and, all as many as had been trusting in him, were disbanded, and came to nothing.
text.KJV: 5:36 For before these dayes rose vp Theudas, boasting himselfe to be some body, to whom a number of men, about foure hundred, ioyned themselues: who was slaine, and all, as many as obeied him, were scattered, & brought to nought.
text.DRV: 5:36 For before these days there rose Theodas, saying he was somebody, to whom consented a number of men about four hundred, who was slain: and all that believed him, were dispersed, and brought to nothing.
text.NABRE: 5:36 Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be someone important, and about four hundred men joined him, but he was killed, and all those who were loyal to him were disbanded and came to nothing.

text.NRSV: After him Judas the Galilean rose up at the time of the census and got people to follow him; he also perished, and all who followed him were scattered.
text.Rotherham: After him, rose up Judas the Galilaean, in the days of the enrolling, and drew a people into revolt after him; and, he, perished, and, all, as many as had trusted in him, were scattered.
text.KJV: 5:37 After this man rose vp Iudas of Galilee, in the dayes of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: hee also perished, and all, euen as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
text.DRV: 5:37 After this fellow there rose Judas of Galilee in the days of the Enrolling, and drew away the people after him, and he perished: and as many as ever consented to him, were dispersed.
text.NABRE: 5:37 After him came Judas the Galilean at the time of the census. He also drew people after him, but he too perished and all who were loyal to him were scattered.

text.NRSV: So in the present case, I tell you, keep away from these men and let them alone; because if this plan or this undertaking is of human origin, it will fail;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I say unto you—stand aloof from these men, and let them alone; because if, of men, be this project or this work, it will be overthrown,—
text.KJV: 5:38 And now I say vnto you, refraine from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsell or this worke be of men, it will come to nought.
text.DRV: 5:38 And now therefore I say to you, depart from these men and let them alone: for if this counsel or work be of men, it will be dissolved:
text.NABRE: 5:38 So now I tell you, have nothing to do with these men, and let them go. For if this endeavor or this activity is of human origin, it will destroy itself.

text.NRSV: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them—in that case you may even be found fighting against God!” They were convinced by him,
text.Rotherham: But, if it is, of God, ye will not be able to overthrow them: lest once, even fighters against God, ye be found.
text.KJV: 5:39 But if it be of God, ye cannot ouerthrow it, lest haply yee be found euen to fight against God.
text.DRV: 5:39 but if it be of God, you are not able to dissolve them, lest perhaps you be found to resist God also. And they consented to him.
text.NABRE: 5:39 But if it comes from God, you will not be able to destroy them; you may even find yourselves fighting against God.” They were persuaded by him.

text.NRSV: and when they had called in the apostles, they had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
text.Rotherham: And they were persuaded by him; and, calling unto them the Apostles, they, with beating, charged them not be speaking upon the name of Jesus; and let them go.
text.KJV: 5:40 And to him they agreed: and when they had called the Apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speake in the Name of Iesus, and let them goe.
text.DRV: 5:40 And calling in the Apostles, after they had scourged them, they charged them that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and dismissed them.
text.NABRE: 5:40 After recalling the apostles, they had them flogged, ordered them to stop speaking in the name of Jesus, and dismissed them.

text.NRSV: As they left the council, they rejoiced that they were considered worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, went rejoicing from the presence of the high-council; in that they had been accounted worthy, in behalf of The Name, to suffer dishonour.
text.KJV: 5:41 ¶ And they departed from the presence of the Councill, reioycing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his Name.
text.DRV: 5:41 And they went from the sight of the Council rejoicing, because they were accounted worthy to suffer reproach for the name of Jesus.
text.NABRE: 5:41 So they left the presence of the Sanhedrin, rejoicing that they had been found worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.

text.NRSV: And every day in the temple and at home they did not cease to teach and proclaim Jesus as the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: And, every day in the temple, and at home, they ceased not to be teaching, and telling the good news as to the Anointed Jesus.
text.KJV: 5:42 And dayly in the Temple, and in euery house, they ceased not to teach and preach Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 5:42 And every day they ceased not in the temple, and from house to house to teach and evangelize Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: 5:42 And all day long, both at the temple and in their homes, they did not stop teaching and proclaiming the Messiah, Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now during those days, when the disciples were increasing in number, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food.
text.Rotherham: But, in these days, the disciples, multiplying, there arose a murmuring of the Grecian Jews against the Hebrews, in that, their widows, were being overlooked in the daily ministry.
text.KJV: 6:1 And in those dayes when the number of the Disciples was multiplied, there arose a murmuring of the Grecians against the Hebrewes, because their widowes were neglected in the daily ministration.
text.DRV: 6:1 And in those days the number of Disciples increasing, there arose a murmuring of the Greeks against the Hebrews, for that their widows were despised in the daily ministry.
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 - The Need for Assistants. At that time, as the number of disciples continued to grow, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution.

text.NRSV: And the twelve called together the whole community of the disciples and said, “It is not right that we should neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables.
text.Rotherham: And the Twelve, calling near the throng of the disciples, said—It doth not seem, right, that, we, forsaking the word of God, should be ministering unto tables.
text.KJV: 6:2 Then the twelue called the multitude of the disciples vnto them, and said, It is not reason that we should leaue the word of God, and serue tables.
text.DRV: 6:2 And the Twelve calling together the multitude of the Disciples, said: it is not reason, that we leave the word of God, and serve tables.
text.NABRE: 6:2 So the Twelve called together the community of the disciples and said, “It is not right for us to neglect the word of God to serve at table.

text.NRSV: Therefore, friends, select from among yourselves seven men of good standing, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint to this task,
text.Rotherham: But look out for yourselves, brethren, seven men from among you, who can be well-attested, full of Spirit and wisdom,—whom we will appoint over this need;
text.KJV: 6:3 Wherefore brethren, looke ye out among you seuen men of honest report, full of the holy Ghost, and wisedome, whom we may appoint ouer this businesse.
text.DRV: 6:3 Consider therefore brethren, seven men of you of good testimony, full of the Holy Ghost and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.
text.NABRE: 6:3 Brothers, select from among you seven reputable men, filled with the Spirit and wisdom, whom we shall appoint to this task,

text.NRSV: while we, for our part, will devote ourselves to prayer and to serving the word.”
text.Rotherham: But, we, unto prayer and unto the ministry of the word, will give constant attention.
text.KJV: 6:4 But we will giue our selues continually to prayer, and to the ministerie of the word.
text.DRV: 6:4 But we will be instant in prayer and the ministry of the word.
text.NABRE: 6:4 whereas we shall devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”

text.NRSV: What they said pleased the whole community, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, together with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch.
text.Rotherham: And the word was pleasing in the sight of all the throng; and they selected Stephen, a man full of faith and Holy Spirit, and Philip and Prochorus and Nicanor and Timon and Parmenas and Nicholaus, a proselyte of Antioch;
text.KJV: 6:5 ¶ And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they chose Steuen, a man full of faith and of the holy Ghost, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Permenas, and Nicolas a proselyte of Antioch.
text.DRV: 6:5 And the saying was liked before all the multitude. And they chose Steven a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost, and Philippe, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a stranger of Antioch:
text.NABRE: 6:5 The proposal was acceptable to the whole community, so they chose Stephen, a man filled with faith and the holy Spirit, also Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicholas of Antioch, a convert to Judaism.

text.NRSV: They had these men stand before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
text.Rotherham: Whom they set before the Apostles, and, praying, they laid upon them their hands.
text.KJV: 6:6 Whom they set before the Apostles: and when they had praied, they layd their hands on them.
text.DRV: 6:6 These they did set in the presence of the Apostles: and praying they imposed hands upon them.
text.NABRE: 6:6 They presented these men to the apostles who prayed and laid hands on them.

text.NRSV: The word of God continued to spread; the number of the disciples increased greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests became obedient to the faith.
text.Rotherham: And, the word of God, went on growing, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem continued to multiply exceedingly; a great multitude of the priests also, were becoming obedient unto the faith.
text.KJV: 6:7 And the word of God encreased, and the number of the Disciples multiplied in Hierusalem greatly, and a great company of the Priests were obedient to the faith.
text.DRV: 6:7 And the word of God increased, and the number of the Disciples was multiplied in Jerusalem exceedingly: a great multitude also of the Priests obeyed the faith.
text.NABRE: 6:7 The word of God continued to spread, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem increased greatly; even a large group of priests were becoming obedient to the faith.

text.NRSV: Stephen, full of grace and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.
text.Rotherham: Now, Stephen, full of favour and power, began to do great wonders and signs among the people.
text.KJV: 6:8 And Steuen full of faith and power, did great wonders and miracles among the people.
text.DRV: 6:8 And Steven full of grace and fortitude did great wonders & signs among the people.
text.NABRE: 6:8 Accusation Against Stephen. Now Stephen, filled with grace and power, was working great wonders and signs among the people.

text.NRSV: Then some of those who belonged to the synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and others of those from Cilicia and Asia, stood up and argued with Stephen.
text.Rotherham: But there rose up certain of those out of the synagogue which is called [the synagogue] of them of Libertium and Cyrene and Alexandria, and certain of them from Cilicia and Asia,—disputing with Stephen;
text.KJV: 6:9 ¶ Then there arose certaine of the Synagogue, which is called the Synagogue of the Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia, and of Asia, disputing with Steuen.
text.DRV: 6:9 And there arose certain of that which is called the Synagogue of the Libertines, and of the Cyrenians, and of the Alexandrians, and of them that were of Cilicia and Asia, disputing with Steven:
text.NABRE: 6:9 Certain members of the so-called Synagogue of Freedmen, Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and people from Cilicia and Asia, came forward and debated with Stephen,

text.NRSV: But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.
text.Rotherham: And they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking,
text.KJV: 6:10 And they were not able to resist the wisedome and the spirit by which he spake.
text.DRV: 6:10 and they could not resist the wisdom and the Spirit that spake.
text.NABRE: 6:10 but they could not withstand the wisdom and the spirit with which he spoke.

text.NRSV: Then they secretly instigated some men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
text.Rotherham: Then, they set on men who were saying—We have heard him speaking profane things against Moses and God.
text.KJV: 6:11 Then they suborned men which said, We haue heard him speake blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
text.DRV: 6:11 Then they suborned men, to say they had heard him speak words of blasphemy against Moyses and God.
text.NABRE: 6:11 Then they instigated some men to say, “We have heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God.”

text.NRSV: They stirred up the people as well as the elders and the scribes; then they suddenly confronted him, seized him, and brought him before the council.
text.Rotherham: And they stirred up the people and the elders and the scribes; and, coming upon him, they caught him away, and led him into the high-council.
text.KJV: 6:12 And they stirred vp the people, and the Elders, and the Scribes, and came vpon him, and caught him, and brought him to the Councell,
text.DRV: 6:12 They therefore stirred up the people, and the Ancients, and the Scribes: and running together they took him, and brought him into the Council,
text.NABRE: 6:12 They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes, accosted him, seized him, and brought him before the Sanhedrin.

text.NRSV: They set up false witnesses who said, “This man never stops saying things against this holy place and the law;
text.Rotherham: And they set up false witnesses, who said—This man, ceaseth not speaking things against this holy place and the law;
text.KJV: 6:13 And set vp false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speake blasphemous words against this holy place, and the Law.
text.DRV: 6:13 and they set false witnesses that said: This man ceaseth not to speak words against the holy place & the Law.
text.NABRE: 6:13 They presented false witnesses who testified, “This man never stops saying things against [this] holy place and the law.

text.NRSV: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses handed on to us.”
text.Rotherham: For we have heard him saying—This Jesus the Nazarene, will overthrow this place, and will change the customs which, Moses, delivered unto us.
text.KJV: 6:14 For we haue heard him say, that this Iesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, & shall change the Customes which Moses deliuered vs.
text.DRV: 6:14 For we have heard him say, that this same Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the traditions, which Moyses delivered unto us.
text.NABRE: 6:14 For we have heard him claim that this Jesus the Nazorean will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.”

text.NRSV: And all who sat in the council looked intently at him, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.
text.Rotherham: And, looking steadfastly at him, all they who were sitting in the high-council, saw his face, as if the face of a messenger;
text.KJV: 6:15 And all that sate in the Councell, looking stedfastly on him, saw his face as it had bene the face of an Angel.
text.DRV: 6:15 And all that sat in the Council beholding him, saw his face as it were the face of an Angel.
text.NABRE: 6:15 All those who sat in the Sanhedrin looked intently at him and saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest asked him, “Are these things so?”
text.Rotherham: And the High-priest said—Are these things, so?
text.KJV: 7:1 Then said the high Priest, Are these things so?
text.DRV: 7:1 And the chief priest said: Are these things so?
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 - Stephen’s Discourses. Then the high priest asked, “Is this so?”

text.NRSV: And Stephen said: “Brothers and fathers, listen to me. The God of glory appeared to our ancestor Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Brethren and fathers, hearken! The God of Glory, appeared unto our father Abraham, while he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Haran,—
text.KJV: 7:2 And hee said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken: The God of glory appeared vnto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
text.DRV: 7:2 Who said: Ye men, brethren and fathers, hear. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before that he abode in Charan,
text.NABRE: 7:2 And he replied, “My brothers and fathers, listen. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was in Mesopotamia, before he had settled in Haran,

text.NRSV: and said to him, ‘Leave your country and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.’
text.Rotherham: And said unto him—Get thee forth, out of thy land, and from among thy kindred, and come unto the land which, unto thee, I will point out.
text.KJV: 7:3 And said vnto him, Get thee out of thy countrey, and from thy kinred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
text.DRV: 7:3 & said to him: Go forth out of thy country, and out of thy kindred, & come into a land that I shall shew thee.
text.NABRE: 7:3 and said to him, ‘Go forth from your land and [from] your kinsfolk to the land that I will show you.’

text.NRSV: Then he left the country of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, God had him move from there to this country in which you are now living.
text.Rotherham: Then, coming forth out of the land of the Chaldeans, he dwelt in Haran; and, from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, in which, ye, now dwell;
text.KJV: 7:4 Then came he out of the land of the Chaldeans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he remoued him into this lande wherein ye now dwell.
text.DRV: 7:4 Then went he forth out of the land of the Chaldees, and dwelt in Charan. And from thence, after his father was dead, he translated him into this land wherein you do now dwell.
text.NABRE: 7:4 So he went forth from the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. And from there, after his father died, he made him migrate to this land where you now dwell.

text.NRSV: He gave him no inheritance in it, not even a foot’s length, but promised to give it to him as his possession and to his descendants after him, even though he had no child.
text.Rotherham: And gave him no inheritance therein, not even a place to set his foot on; and yet promised to give it unto him in possession, and unto his seed after him, when as yet he had not a child.
text.KJV: 7:5 And he gaue him none inheritance in it, no not so much as to set his foote on: yet he promised that he would giue it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.
text.DRV: 7:5 And he gave him no inheritance in it, no not the pace of a foot: and he promised to give it him in possession, and to his seed after him, when as he had no child.
text.NABRE: 7:5 Yet he gave him no inheritance in it, not even a foot’s length, but he did promise to give it to him and his descendants as a possession, even though he was childless.

text.NRSV: And God spoke in these terms, that his descendants would be resident aliens in a country belonging to others, who would enslave them and mistreat them during four hundred years.
text.Rotherham: But God, spake thus—His seed shall be a sojourner in a foreign land, and they will bring it into bondage, and ill-treat it four-hundred years;
text.KJV: 7:6 And God spake on this wise, that his seede should soiourne in a strange land, and that they should bring them into bondage, and intreate them euill foure hundreth yeeres.
text.DRV: 7:6 And God spake to him: That his seed shall be a sojourner in a strange country, and they shall subdue them to servitude, and shall evil entreat them four hundred years.
text.NABRE: 7:6 And God spoke thus, ‘His descendants shall be aliens in a land not their own, where they shall be enslaved and oppressed for four hundred years;

text.NRSV: ‘But I will judge the nation that they serve,’ said God, ‘and after that they shall come out and worship me in this place.’
text.Rotherham: And, the nation unto which they shall be in bondage, will, I, judge—said God; and, after these things, shall they come forth, and render divine service unto me, in this place.
text.KJV: 7:7 And the nation to whom they shal bee in bondage, will I iudge, saide God: And after that shall they come forth, and serue me in this place.
text.DRV: 7:7 and the nation which they shall serve, will I judge, said God. And after these things they shall go forth and shall serve me in this place.
text.NABRE: 7:7 but I will bring judgment on the nation they serve,’ God said, ‘and after that they will come out and worship me in this place.’

text.NRSV: Then he gave him the covenant of circumcision. And so Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day; and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
text.Rotherham: And he gave unto him a covenant of circumcision; and, thus, he begat Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, and Isaac, Jacob, and Jacob, the twelve patriarchs.
text.KJV: 7:8 And he gaue him the couenant of Circumcision: and so Abraham begate Isaac, and circumcised him the eight day: and Isaac begate Iacob, and Iacob begate the twelue Patriarchs.
text.DRV: 7:8 And he gave him the testament of circumcision, and so he begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day: and Isaac, Jacob: and Jacob, the twelve Patriarchs.
text.NABRE: 7:8 Then he gave him the covenant of circumcision, and so he became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, as Isaac did Jacob, and Jacob the twelve patriarchs.

text.NRSV: “The patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; but God was with him,
text.Rotherham: And, the patriarchs, being jealous of Joseph, gave him up into Egypt; and, God, was, with him,
text.KJV: 7:9 And the Patriarchs moued with enuie, sold Ioseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
text.DRV: 7:9 And the Patriarchs through emulation, sold Joseph into Ægypt. And God was with him:
text.NABRE: 7:9 “And the patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into slavery in Egypt; but God was with him

text.NRSV: and rescued him from all his afflictions, and enabled him to win favor and to show wisdom when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.
text.Rotherham: And rescued him out of all his tribulations, and gave him favour and wisdom before Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he appointed him governor over Egypt and all his house.
text.KJV: 7:10 And deliuered him out of all his afflictions, and gaue him fauour and wisedome in the sight of Pharao king of Egypt: and he made him gouernour ouer Egypt and all his house.
text.DRV: 7:10 and delivered him out of all his tribulations. And he gave him grace and wisdom in the sight of Pharao the king of Ægypt, and he appointed him Governor over Ægypt and over all his house.
text.NABRE: 7:10 and rescued him from all his afflictions. He granted him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, who put him in charge of Egypt and [of] his entire household.

text.NRSV: Now there came a famine throughout Egypt and Canaan, and great suffering, and our ancestors could find no food.
text.Rotherham: And there came a famine upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great tribulation, and our fathers could not find pasture.
text.KJV: 7:11 Now there came a dearth ouer all the land of Egypt, and Chanaan, and great affliction, and our fathers found no sustenance.
text.DRV: 7:11 And there came famine upon all Ægypt and Chanaan, and great tribulation: and our fathers found no victuals.
text.NABRE: 7:11 Then a famine and great affliction struck all Egypt and Canaan, and our ancestors could find no food;

text.NRSV: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there on their first visit.
text.Rotherham: But Jacob, hearing there was corn in Egypt, sent off our fathers, first;
text.KJV: 7:12 But when Iacob heard that there was corne in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
text.DRV: 7:12 But when Jacob had heard that there was corn in Ægypt; he sent our fathers first:
text.NABRE: 7:12 but when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there a first time.

text.NRSV: On the second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And, the second time, Joseph was made known unto his brethren, and, the race of Joseph was made, manifest, unto Pharaoh.
text.KJV: 7:13 And at the second time Ioseph was made knowen to his brethren, and Iosephs kinred was made knowen vnto Pharao.
text.DRV: 7:13 and at the second time Joseph was known of his brethren, and his kindred was made known unto Pharao.
text.NABRE: 7:13 The second time, Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph sent and invited his father Jacob and all his relatives to come to him, seventy-five in all;
text.Rotherham: And Joseph, sending forth, called for Jacob his father, and all the kindred, consisting of seventy-five souls;
text.KJV: 7:14 Then sent Ioseph, and called his father Iacob to him, and all his kinred, threescore and fifteeene soules.
text.DRV: 7:14 And Joseph sending, called thither Jacob his father and all his kindred in seventy five souls.
text.NABRE: 7:14 Then Joseph sent for his father Jacob, inviting him and his whole clan, seventy-five persons;

text.NRSV: so Jacob went down to Egypt. He himself died there as well as our ancestors,
text.Rotherham: And Jacob went down [into Egypt]. And he died, he, and our fathers;
text.KJV: 7:15 So Iacob went downe into Egypt, and died, he and our fathers,
text.DRV: 7:15 And Jacob descended into Ægypt: and he died, and our fathers.
text.NABRE: 7:15 and Jacob went down to Egypt. And he and our ancestors died

text.NRSV: and their bodies were brought back to Shechem and laid in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
text.Rotherham: And were brought over into Shechem, and laid in the tomb which Abraham had purchased, for a price of silver, of the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
text.KJV: 7:16 And were caried ouer into Sichem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a summe of money of the sonnes of Emor the father of Sichem.
text.DRV: 7:16 And they were translated into Sichem, and were laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a price of silver of the sons of Hemor the son of Sichem.
text.NABRE: 7:16 and were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a sum of money from the sons of Hamor at Shechem.

text.NRSV: “But as the time drew near for the fulfillment of the promise that God had made to Abraham, our people in Egypt increased and multiplied
text.Rotherham: Now, just as the time of the promise was drawing near wherewith God had agreed with Abraham, the people grew, and were multiplied in Egypt,—
text.KJV: 7:17 But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworne to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
text.DRV: 7:17 And when the time drew near of the promise which God had promised to Abraham, the people increased and was multiplied in Ægypt,
text.NABRE: 7:17 “When the time drew near for the fulfillment of the promise that God pledged to Abraham, the people had increased and become very numerous in Egypt,

text.NRSV: until another king who had not known Joseph ruled over Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Until there arose another sort of king over Egypt, who had not known Joseph.
text.KJV: 7:18 Till another king arose, which knew not Ioseph.
text.DRV: 7:18 until another king arose in Ægypt, that knew not Joseph.
text.NABRE: 7:18 until another king who knew nothing of Joseph came to power [in Egypt].

text.NRSV: He dealt craftily with our race and forced our ancestors to abandon their infants so that they would die.
text.Rotherham: The same, dealing craftily with our race, ill-treated our fathers, so as to cause their babes to be exposed, to the end they might not be suffered to live.
text.KJV: 7:19 The same dealt subtilly with our kinred, and euill intreated our fathers, so that they cast out their yong children, to the end they might not liue.
text.DRV: 7:19 This same circumventing our stock, afflicted our fathers, that they should expose their children, to the end they might not be kept alive.
text.NABRE: 7:19 He dealt shrewdly with our people and oppressed [our] ancestors by forcing them to expose their infants, that they might not survive.

text.NRSV: At this time Moses was born, and he was beautiful before God. For three months he was brought up in his father’s house;
text.Rotherham: In which season, Moses, was born, and was exceeding goodly,—who was nourished up three months in the house of his father;
text.KJV: 7:20 In which time Moses was borne, and was exceeding faire, and nourished vp in his fathers house three moneths:
text.DRV: 7:20 The same time was Moyses born, and he was acceptable to God; who was nourished three months in his father's house.
text.NABRE: 7:20 At this time Moses was born, and he was extremely beautiful. For three months he was nursed in his father’s house;

text.NRSV: and when he was abandoned, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
text.Rotherham: But, when he was exposed, the daughter of Pharaoh rescued him, and nourished him for herself, as a son.
text.KJV: 7:21 And when he was cast out, Pharaohs daughter tooke him vp, and nourished him for her owne sonne.
text.DRV: 7:21 And when he was exposed, Pharao's daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
text.NABRE: 7:21 but when he was exposed, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.

text.NRSV: So Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.
text.Rotherham: And, so Moses was trained in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in his words and works.
text.KJV: 7:22 And Moses was learned in all the wisedome of the Egyptians, and was mightie in words and in deeds.
text.DRV: 7:22 And Moyses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Ægyptians: and he was mighty in his words and works.
text.NABRE: 7:22 Moses was educated [in] all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.

text.NRSV: “When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his relatives, the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And, when there was being fulfilled unto him a period of forty years, it came up on his heart to look after his brethren the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 7:23 And when he was full forty yeres old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 7:23 And when he was fully of the age of forty years, it came to his mind to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 7:23 “When he was forty years old, he decided to visit his kinsfolk, the Israelites.

text.NRSV: When he saw one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.
text.Rotherham: And, seeing one being wronged, he defended him, and avenged him that was getting worn out, smiting the Egyptian.
text.KJV: 7:24 And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and auenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
text.DRV: 7:24 And when he had seen one suffer wrong, he defended him: and striking the Ægyptian, he revenged his quarrel that sustained the wrong.
text.NABRE: 7:24 When he saw one of them treated unjustly, he defended and avenged the oppressed man by striking down the Egyptian.

text.NRSV: He supposed that his kinsfolk would understand that God through him was rescuing them, but they did not understand.
text.Rotherham: But he supposed his brethren would understand that, God, through his hand, would give them deliverance; whereas they understood not.
text.KJV: 7:25 For he supposed his brethren would haue vnderstood, how that God by his hand would deliuer them, but they vnderstood not.
text.DRV: 7:25 And he thought that his brethren did understand that God by his hand would save them: but they understood it not.
text.NABRE: 7:25 He assumed [his] kinsfolk would understand that God was offering them deliverance through him, but they did not understand.

text.NRSV: The next day he came to some of them as they were quarreling and tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers; why do you wrong each other?’
text.Rotherham: On the following day also, he appeared unto them, as they were contending, and would have reconciled them in peace, saying—Men! ye are, brothers! Wherefore wrong ye one another?
text.KJV: 7:26 And the next day he shewed himselfe vnto them as they stroue, and would haue set them at one againe, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren, Why doe yee wrong one to another?
text.DRV: 7:26 And the day following he appeared to them being at strife: and he reconciled them unto peace, saying: Men, ye are brethren, wherefore hurt you one another?
text.NABRE: 7:26 The next day he appeared to them as they were fighting and tried to reconcile them peacefully, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you harming one another?’

text.NRSV: But the man who was wronging his neighbor pushed him aside, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?
text.Rotherham: But, he that was wronging his neighbour, thrust him away, saying—Who hath appointed, thee, to be ruler and judge over us?
text.KJV: 7:27 But hee that did his neighbour wrong, thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a Iudge ouer vs?
text.DRV: 7:27 But he that did the injury to his neighbour, repelled him, saying: Who hath appointed thee prince and judge over us:
text.NABRE: 7:27 Then the one who was harming his neighbor pushed him aside, saying, ‘Who appointed you ruler and judge over us?

text.NRSV: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
text.Rotherham: Art, thou, wishing, to kill me, in the same way thou didst kill, yesterday, the Egyptian?
text.KJV: 7:28 Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
text.DRV: 7:28 What, wilt thou kill me, as thou didst yesterday kill the Ægyptian?
text.NABRE: 7:28 Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

text.NRSV: When he heard this, Moses fled and became a resident alien in the land of Midian. There he became the father of two sons.
text.Rotherham: And Moses fled at this saying, and became a sojourner in the land of Midian, where he begat two sons.
text.KJV: 7:29 Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begate two sonnes.
text.DRV: 7:29 And Moyses fled upon this word: and he became a sojourner in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
text.NABRE: 7:29 Moses fled when he heard this and settled as an alien in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.

text.NRSV: “Now when forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.
text.Rotherham: And, when forty years were fulfilled, there appeared unto him, in the desert of Mount Sinai, a messenger, in a flame of fire, in a bush.
text.KJV: 7:30 And when fourtie yeeres were expired, there appeared to him in the wildernes of mount Sina, an Angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
text.DRV: 7:30 And after forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the desert of mount Sina an Angel in the fire of the flame of a bush.
text.NABRE: 7:30 “Forty years later, an angel appeared to him in the desert near Mount Sinai in the flame of a burning bush.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight; and as he approached to look, there came the voice of the Lord:
text.Rotherham: And, Moses, seeing it, marvelled at the sight; and, as he was going near to observe, there came a voice of the Lord—
text.KJV: 7:31 When Moses saw it, he wondred at the sight: and as he drew neere to behold it, the voyce of the Lord came vnto him,
text.DRV: 7:31 And Moyses seeing it, marveled at the vision. And as he went near to view it, the voice of our Lord was made to him:
text.NABRE: 7:31 When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight, and as he drew near to look at it, the voice of the Lord came,

text.NRSV: ‘I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look.
text.Rotherham: I, am the God of thy fathers, The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. And Moses becoming, terrified, durst not observe.
text.KJV: 7:32 Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Iacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
text.DRV: 7:32 I am the God of thy fathers; the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moyses being made to tremble, durst not view it.
text.NABRE: 7:32 ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob.’ Then Moses, trembling, did not dare to look at it.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said unto him—Loose the sandals of thy feet; for, the place whereon thou art standing, is, hallowed ground.
text.KJV: 7:33 Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shooes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest, is holy ground.
text.DRV: 7:33 And our Lord said to him: Loose off the shoe of thy feet, for the place wherein thou standest, is holy ground.
text.NABRE: 7:33 But the Lord said to him, ‘Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you stand is holy ground.

text.NRSV: I have surely seen the mistreatment of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Come now, I will send you to Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: I have, indeed seen, the ill-treatment of my people that is in Egypt, and, unto their groaning, have I hearkened, and have come down to rescue them. Now, therefore, come! I will send thee into Egypt.
text.KJV: 7:34 I haue seene, I haue seene the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I haue heard their groning, & am come downe to deliuer them: And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
