text.DRV: 7:34 Seeing I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Ægypt, and I have heard their groaning and am descended to deliver them. And now come, and I will send thee into Ægypt.
text.NABRE: 7:34 I have witnessed the affliction of my people in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Come now, I will send you to Egypt.’

text.NRSV: “It was this Moses whom they rejected when they said, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’ whom God now sent as both ruler and liberator through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
text.Rotherham: The same Moses whom they had refused, saying—Who hath appointed thee to be ruler and judge? the same, both as ruler and redeemer, did God send, by the hand of the messenger who had appeared unto him in the bush:
text.KJV: 7:35 This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a Iudge? the same did God send to bee a ruler and a deliuerer, by the handes of the Angel which appeared to him in the bush.
text.DRV: 7:35 This Moyses, whom they denied, saying: Who hath appointed thee prince and Captain? him God sent prince & redeemer with the hand of the Angel that appeared to him in the bush.
text.NABRE: 7:35 This Moses, whom they had rejected with the words, ‘Who appointed you ruler and judge?’ God sent as [both] ruler and deliverer, through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

text.NRSV: He led them out, having performed wonders and signs in Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
text.Rotherham: The same, led them forth, doing wonders and signs in Egypt, and in the Red Sea, and in the desert, forty years:
text.KJV: 7:36 He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signes in the land of Egypt, and in the red Sea, and in the wildernesse fortie yeeres.
text.DRV: 7:36 He brought them forth doing wonders and signs in the land of Ægypt, and in the red sea, and in the desert forty years.
text.NABRE: 7:36 This man led them out, performing wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the desert for forty years.

text.NRSV: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up a prophet for you from your own people as he raised me up.’
text.Rotherham: The same, is the Moses that said unto the sons of Israel—A prophet, unto you, will God raise up, from among your brethren, like unto me:
text.KJV: 7:37 ¶ This is that Moses which said vnto the children of Israel, A Prophet shall the Lord your God raise vp vnto you of your brethren, like vnto mee: him shall ye heare.
text.DRV: 7:37 This is that Moyses which said to the children of Israel: A Prophet will God raise up to you of your own brethren as myself: him you shall hear.
text.NABRE: 7:37 It was this Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you, from among your own kinsfolk, a prophet like me.’

text.NRSV: He is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors; and he received living oracles to give to us.
text.Rotherham: The same, is he that came to be in the assembly in the desert, with the messenger who was speaking with him in Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, he who welcomed living utterances, to give unto us:
text.KJV: 7:38 This is he that was in þ Church in the wildernesse with the Angel, which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who receiued the liuely oracles, to giue vnto vs.
text.DRV: 7:38 This is he that was in the assembly in the wilderness, with the Angel that spake to him in Mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the words of life to give unto us.
text.NABRE: 7:38 It was he who, in the assembly in the desert, was with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai and with our ancestors, and he received living utterances to hand on to us.

text.NRSV: Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him; instead, they pushed him aside, and in their hearts they turned back to Egypt,
text.Rotherham: Unto whom, our fathers would not become obedient, but thrust him away, and turned in their hearts unto Egypt,
text.KJV: 7:39 To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned backe againe into Egypt,
text.DRV: 7:39 To whom our fathers would not be obedient: but they repelled him, and in their hearts turned away into Ægypt,
text.NABRE: 7:39 “Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him; instead, they pushed him aside and in their hearts turned back to Egypt,

text.NRSV: saying to Aaron, ‘Make gods for us who will lead the way for us; as for this Moses who led us out from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’
text.Rotherham: Saying unto Aaron—Make as gods who shall go before us; for, as for this Moses who brought us forth out of Egypt, we know not what hath befallen him!
text.KJV: 7:40 Saying vnto Aaron, Make vs gods to goe before vs. For as for this Moses, which brought vs out of the land of Egypt, we wote not what is become of him.
text.DRV: 7:40 saying to Aaron: Make us gods that may go before us. For this Moyses that brought us out of the land of Ægypt, we know not what is befallen to him.
text.NABRE: 7:40 saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods who will be our leaders. As for that Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’

text.NRSV: At that time they made a calf, offered a sacrifice to the idol, and reveled in the works of their hands.
text.Rotherham: And so they fell to calf-making in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.
text.KJV: 7:41 And they made a calfe in those dayes, and offered sacrifice vnto the idole, and reioyced in the workes of their owne hands.
text.DRV: 7:41 And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
text.NABRE: 7:41 So they made a calf in those days, offered sacrifice to the idol, and reveled in the works of their hands.

text.NRSV: But God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: ‘Did you offer to me slain victims and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
text.Rotherham: But God, turned, and delivered them up to be doing divine service unto the host of heaven,—just as it is written in a book of the prophets—Victims and sacrifices did ye offer unto me, forty years in the desert, O house of Israel?
text.KJV: 7:42 Then God turned, and gaue them vp to worship the hoste of heauen, as it is written in the booke of the Prophets, O ye house of Israel, haue ye offered to me slaine beasts, and sacrifices, by the space of fourty yeeres in the wildernesse?
text.DRV: 7:42 And God turned, and delivered them up to serve the host of Heaven, as it is written in the book of the Prophets: Did you offer victims and hosts unto me forty years in the desert, O house of Israel?
text.NABRE: 7:42 Then God turned and handed them over to worship the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: ‘Did you bring me sacrifices and offerings for forty years in the desert, O house of Israel?

text.NRSV: No; you took up the tent of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made to worship; so I will remove you beyond Babylon.’
text.Rotherham: Nay! but ye took with you the tent of Moloch, and the star of the god Rephan,—the forms which ye made to bow down unto them: therefore will I carry you away beyond Babylon.
text.KJV: 7:43 Yea, ye tooke vp the Tabernacle of Moloch, and the starre of your God Remphan, figures which ye made, to worship them: and I will carie you away beyond Babylon.
text.DRV: 7:43 And you took unto you the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Rempham, figures which you made, to adore them. And I will translate you beyond Babylon.
text.NABRE: 7:43 No, you took up the tent of Moloch and the star of [your] god Rephan, the images that you made to worship. So I shall take you into exile beyond Babylon.’

text.NRSV: “Our ancestors had the tent of testimony in the wilderness, as God directed when he spoke to Moses, ordering him to make it according to the pattern he had seen.
text.Rotherham: The tent of witness, was with our fathers in the desert, according as he who was speaking unto Moses, gave instructions, to make it, according to the model which he had seen.
text.KJV: 7:44 Our fathers had the Tabernacle of witnesse in the wildernesse, as hee had appointed, speaking vnto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seene.
text.DRV: 7:44 The tabernacle of testimony was among our fathers in the desert, as God ordained speaking to Moyses, that he should make it according to the form which he had seen.
text.NABRE: 7:44 Our ancestors had the tent of testimony in the desert just as the One who spoke to Moses directed him to make it according to the pattern he had seen.

text.NRSV: Our ancestors in turn brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out before our ancestors. And it was there until the time of David,
text.Rotherham: Which our fathers succeeding to, also brought in, with Joshua, when taking possession of the nations, which God put out from before our fathers until the days of David;
text.KJV: 7:45 Which also our fathers that came after, brought in with Iesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God draue out before the face of our fathers, vnto the dayes of Dauid,
text.DRV: 7:45 Which our fathers with Jesus receiving, brought it in also into the possession of the Gentiles. Which God expelled from the face of our fathers, till in the days of David,
text.NABRE: 7:45 Our ancestors who inherited it brought it with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out from before our ancestors, up to the time of David,

text.NRSV: who found favor with God and asked that he might find a dwelling place for the house of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Who found favour before God, and asked to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.
text.KJV: 7:46 Who found fauour before God, and desired to find a Tabernacle for the God of Iacob.
text.DRV: 7:46 who found grace before God, and desired that he might find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
text.NABRE: 7:46 who found favor in the sight of God and asked that he might find a dwelling place for the house of Jacob.

text.NRSV: But it was Solomon who built a house for him.
text.Rotherham: But, Solomon, built him a house.
text.KJV: 7:47 But Solomon built him an house.
text.DRV: 7:47 And Salomon built him a house.
text.NABRE: 7:47 But Solomon built a house for him.

text.NRSV: Yet the Most High does not dwell in houses made with human hands; as the prophet says,
text.Rotherham: Although indeed, the Most High, not in hand-made places, dwelleth; just as, the prophet, saith—
text.KJV: 7:48 Howbeit the most high dwelleth not in temples made with hands, as saith the Prophet,
text.DRV: 7:48 But the Highest dwelleth not in houses made by hand, as the Prophet saith:
text.NABRE: 7:48 Yet the Most High does not dwell in houses made by human hands. As the prophet says:

text.NRSV: ‘Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is the place of my rest?
text.Rotherham: Heaven is my thrown, and, the earth, is my footstool: What manner of house, will ye build me, saith the Lord,—Or what shall be the place of my resting?
text.KJV: 7:49 Heauen is my throne, and earth is my footestoole: What house will ye build me, saith the Lord? Or what is the place of my rest?
text.DRV: 7:49 Heaven is my seat: and the earth the foot-stool of my feet. What house will you build me, saith our Lord, or what place is there of my resting?
text.NABRE: 7:49 ‘The heavens are my throne, the earth is my footstool. What kind of house can you build for me? says the Lord, or what is to be my resting place?

text.NRSV: Did not my hand make all these things?’
text.Rotherham: Hath not, my hand, made all these things?
text.KJV: 7:50 Hath not my hand made all these things?
text.DRV: 7:50 Hath not my hand made all these things?
text.NABRE: 7:50 Did not my hand make all these things?’

text.NRSV: “You stiff-necked people, uncircumcised in heart and ears, you are always opposing the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors used to do.
text.Rotherham: Ye stiff-necked and uncircumcised in hearts and in ears, ye, always, against the Holy Spirit, do strive! As your fathers, ye also:
text.KJV: 7:51 ¶ Ye stifnecked and vncircumcised in heart, and eares, ye doe alwayes resist the holy Ghost? as your fathers did, so doe ye.
text.DRV: 7:51 You stiff-necked and of uncircumcised hearts and ears, you always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers, yourselves also.
text.NABRE: 7:51 Conclusion. “You stiff-necked people, uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always oppose the holy Spirit; you are just like your ancestors.

text.NRSV: Which of the prophets did your ancestors not persecute? They killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, and now you have become his betrayers and murderers.
text.Rotherham: Which of the prophets, did not your fathers persecute? Yea they slew them who declared beforehand concerning the coming of the Righteous One—of whom, now, ye, have become, betrayers and murderers!
text.KJV: 7:52 Which of the Prophets haue not your fathers persecuted? And they haue slaine them which shewed before of the comming of the Iust one, of whom ye haue bene now the betrayers and murderers:
text.DRV: 7:52 Which of the Prophets did not your fathers persecute? And they slew them that foretold of the coming of the Just one, of whom now
text.NABRE: 7:52 Which of the prophets did your ancestors not persecute? They put to death those who foretold the coming of the righteous one, whose betrayers and murderers you have now become.

text.NRSV: You are the ones that received the law as ordained by angels, and yet you have not kept it.”
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, received the law through ranks of messengers,—and guarded it not.
text.KJV: 7:53 Who haue receiued the Lawe by the disposition of Angels, and haue not kept it.
text.DRV: 7:53 you have been betrayers and murderers: who received the Law by the disposition of Angels, and have not kept it.
text.NABRE: 7:53 You received the law as transmitted by angels, but you did not observe it.”

text.NRSV: When they heard these things, they became enraged and ground their teeth at Stephen.
text.Rotherham: And, while they were hearing these things, they were being pierced in their hearts, and began gnashing their teeth against him.
text.KJV: 7:54 ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.
text.DRV: 7:54 And hearing these things they were cut in their hearts, and they gnashed with their teeth at him.
text.NABRE: 7:54 Stephen’s Martyrdom. When they heard this, they were infuriated, and they ground their teeth at him.

text.NRSV: But filled with the Holy Spirit, he gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
text.Rotherham: But he, being already full of Holy Spirit, looking steadfastly into heaven, saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God;
text.KJV: 7:55 But hee being full of the holy Ghost, looked vp stedfastly into heauen, and saw the glory of God, and Iesus standing on the right hand of God,
text.DRV: 7:55 But he being full of the Holy Ghost, looking steadfastly unto Heaven, saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God. And he said: Behold I see the Heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.
text.NABRE: 7:55 But he, filled with the holy Spirit, looked up intently to heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God,

text.NRSV: “Look,” he said, “I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”
text.Rotherham: And said—Lo! I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man, standing, on the right hand of God.
text.KJV: 7:56 And said, Behold, I see the heauens opened, and the Sonne of man standing on the right hand of God.
text.DRV: 7:56 And they crying out with a loud voice, stopped their ears, and with one accord ran violently upon him.
text.NABRE: 7:56 and he said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”

text.NRSV: But they covered their ears, and with a loud shout all rushed together against him.
text.Rotherham: And, crying out with a loud voice, they held their ears, and rushed with one accord upon him;
text.KJV: 7:57 Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their eares, and ran vpon him with one accord,
text.DRV: 7:57 And casting him forth without the city they stoned him: and the witnesses laid off their garments beside the feet of a young man that was called Saul.
text.NABRE: 7:57 But they cried out in a loud voice, covered their ears, and rushed upon him together.

text.NRSV: Then they dragged him out of the city and began to stone him; and the witnesses laid their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text.Rotherham: And, thrusting him forth outside the city, proceeded to stone him. And, the witnesses, laid their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text.KJV: 7:58 And cast him out of the citie, and stoned him: and the witnesses layd downe their clothes at a yong mans feete, whose name was Saul.
text.DRV: 7:58 And they stoned Steven invocating, and saying: Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
text.NABRE: 7:58 They threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses laid down their cloaks at the feet of a young man named Saul.

text.NRSV: While they were stoning Stephen, he prayed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
text.Rotherham: And they stoned Stephen, as he was invoking and saying—Lord Jesus! give welcome unto my spirit.
text.KJV: 7:59 And they stoned Steuen, calling vpon God, and saying, Lord Iesus receiue my spirit.
text.DRV: 7:59 And falling on his knees, he cried with a loud voice, saying: Lord, lay not this sin unto them. And when he had said this, he fell asleep. And Saul was consenting to his death.
text.NABRE: 7:59 As they were stoning Stephen, he called out, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”

text.NRSV: Then he knelt down and cried out in a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” When he had said this, he died.
text.Rotherham: And, kneeling down, he cried out with a loud voice—Lord! do not charge against them, this sin. And, having said this, he fell asleep.
text.KJV: 7:60 And he kneeled downe, and cried with a loud voice, Lord lay not this sinne to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleepe.
text.NABRE: 7:60 Then he fell to his knees and cried out in a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them”; and when he said this, he fell asleep.

text.Base: And Saul approved of their killing him. That day a severe persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout the countryside of Judea and Samaria.
text.KJV: 8:1 And Saul was consenting vnto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the Church which was at Hierusalem, and they were all scattered abroad through out the regions of Iudea, and Samaria, except the Apostles.
text.DRV: 8:1 And the same day there was made a great persecution in the Church, which was at Jerusalem, and all were dispersed through the countries of Jewry and Samaria, saving the Apostles.
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 - Now Saul was consenting to his execution. Persecution of the Church. On that day, there broke out a severe persecution of the church in Jerusalem, and all were scattered throughout the countryside of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.

text.Base: Devout men buried Stephen and made loud lamentation over him.
text.KJV: 8:2 And deuout men carried Steuen to his buriall, and made great lamentation ouer him.
text.DRV: 8:2 And devout men took order for Steven's funeral, and made great mourning upon him.
text.NABRE: 8:2 Devout men buried Stephen and made a loud lament over him.

text.Base: But Saul was ravaging the church by entering house after house; dragging off both men and women, he committed them to prison.
text.KJV: 8:3 As for Saul, he made hauocke of the Church, entring into euery house, and hailing men and women, committed them to prison.
text.DRV: 8:3 But Saul wasted the Church: entering in from house to house, and drawing men and women, delivered them into prison.
text.NABRE: 8:3 Saul, meanwhile, was trying to destroy the church; entering house after house and dragging out men and women, he handed them over for imprisonment.

text.Base: Now those who were scattered went from place to place, proclaiming the word.
text.KJV: 8:4 Therefore they that were scattered abroad, went euery where preaching the word.
text.DRV: 8:4 They therefore that were dispersed, passed through, evangelizing the word.
text.NABRE: 8:4 III. The Mission in Judea and Samaria - Philip in Samaria. Now those who had been scattered went about preaching the word.

text.Base: Philip went down to the city of Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them.
text.KJV: 8:5 Then Philip went downe to the citie of Samaria, and preached Christ vnto them.
text.DRV: 8:5 And Philippe descending into the city of Samaria, preached Christ unto them.
text.NABRE: 8:5 Thus Philip went down to [the] city of Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them.

text.Base: The crowds with one mind paid attention to what was said by Philip, when they heard him and saw the signs that he was doing.
text.KJV: 8:6 And the people with one accord gaue heed vnto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
text.DRV: 8:6 and the multitudes were attent to those things which were said of Philippe, with one accord hearing, and seeing the signs that he did.
text.NABRE: 8:6 With one accord, the crowds paid attention to what was said by Philip when they heard it and saw the signs he was doing.

text.Base: For unclean spirits, crying with loud shrieks, came out of many who were possessed; and many others who were paralyzed or lame were cured.
text.KJV: 8:7 For vncleane spirits, crying with lowd voyce, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.
text.DRV: 8:7 For many of them that had unclean spirits, crying with a loud voice, went out. And many sick of the palsy and lame were cured.
text.NABRE: 8:7 For unclean spirits, crying out in a loud voice, came out of many possessed people, and many paralyzed and crippled people were cured.

text.Base: So there was great joy in that city.
text.KJV: 8:8 And there was great ioy in that citie.
text.DRV: 8:8 There was made therefore great joy in that city.
text.NABRE: 8:8 There was great joy in that city.

text.Base: Now a certain man named Simon had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, saying that he was someone great.
text.KJV: 8:9 But there was a certaine man called Simon, which before time in the same citie vsed sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giuing out that himselfe was some great one.
text.DRV: 8:9 And a certain man named Simon, who before had been in that city a Magician, seducing the nation of Samaria, saying himself to be some great one:
text.NABRE: 8:9 Simon the Magician. A man named Simon used to practice magic in the city and astounded the people of Samaria, claiming to be someone great.

text.Base: All of them, from the least to the greatest, listened to him eagerly, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called Great.”
text.KJV: 8:10 To whom they all gaue heed from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
text.DRV: 8:10 unto whom all harkened from the least to the greatest, saying: This man is the power of God, that is called great.
text.NABRE: 8:10 All of them, from the least to the greatest, paid attention to him, saying, “This man is the ‘Power of God’ that is called ‘Great.’”

text.Base: And they listened eagerly to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
text.KJV: 8:11 And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.
text.DRV: 8:11 And they were attent upon him, because a long time he had bewitched them with his magical practices.
text.NABRE: 8:11 They paid attention to him because he had astounded them by his magic for a long time,

text.Base: But when they believed Philip, who was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
text.KJV: 8:12 But when they beleeued Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdome of God, and the Name of Iesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
text.DRV: 8:12 But when they had believed Philippe evangelizing of the Kingdom of God, and of the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, men and women.
text.NABRE: 8:12 but once they began to believe Philip as he preached the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, men and women alike were baptized.

text.Base: Even Simon himself believed. After being baptized, he stayed close to Philip and was amazed when he saw the signs and great miracles that were taking place.
text.KJV: 8:13 Then Simon himselfe beleeued also: and when hee was baptized, hee continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signes which were done.
text.DRV: 8:13 Then Simon also himself believed: & being baptized, he cleaved to Philippe. Seeing also signs and very great miracles to be done, he was astonished with admiration.
text.NABRE: 8:13 Even Simon himself believed and, after being baptized, became devoted to Philip; and when he saw the signs and mighty deeds that were occurring, he was astounded.

text.Base: Now when the apostles at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
text.KJV: 8:14 Now when the Apostles which were at Hierusalem, heard that Samaria had receiued the word of God, they sent vnto them Peter and Iohn.
text.DRV: 8:14 And when the Apostles who were in Jerusalem, had heard that Samaria had received the word of God: they sent unto them Peter and John.
text.NABRE: 8:14 Now when the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent them Peter and John,

text.Base: The two went down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit
text.KJV: 8:15 Who when they were come downe, praied for them that they might receiue the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 8:15 Who when they were come, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 8:15 who went down and prayed for them, that they might receive the holy Spirit,

text.Base: (for as yet the Spirit had not come upon any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus).
text.KJV: 8:16 (For as yet hee was fallen vpon none of them: onely they were baptized in the Name of the Lord Iesus.)
text.DRV: 8:16 For he was not yet come upon any of them, but they were only baptized in the name of our Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: 8:16 for it had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

text.Base: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 8:17 Then layde they their hands on them, and they receiued the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 8:17 Then did they impose their hands upon them, & they received the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 8:17 Then they laid hands on them and they received the holy Spirit.

text.Base: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,
text.KJV: 8:18 And when Simon saw that through laying on of the Apostles hands, the holy Ghost was giuen, hee offered them money,
text.DRV: 8:18 And when Simon had seen that by the imposition of the hand of the Apostles, the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
text.NABRE: 8:18 When Simon saw that the Spirit was conferred by the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money

text.Base: saying, “Give me also this power so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
text.KJV: 8:19 Saying, Giue me also this power, that on whomsoeuer I lay handes, hee may receiue the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 8:19 saying: Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I impose my hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 8:19 and said, “Give me this power too, so that anyone upon whom I lay my hands may receive the holy Spirit.”

text.Base: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain God’s gift with money!
text.KJV: 8:20 But Peter said vnto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.
text.DRV: 8:20 But Peter said to him: Thy money be with thee unto perdition: because thou hast thought that the gift of God is purchased with money.
text.NABRE: 8:20 But Peter said to him, “May your money perish with you, because you thought that you could buy the gift of God with money.

text.Base: You have no part or share in this, for your heart is not right before God.
text.KJV: 8:21 Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter, for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
text.DRV: 8:21 Thou hast no part, nor lot in this word. For thy heart is not right before God.
text.NABRE: 8:21 You have no share or lot in this matter, for your heart is not upright before God.

text.Base: Repent therefore of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you.
text.KJV: 8:22 Repent therefore of this thy wickednesse, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiuen thee.
text.DRV: 8:22 Do penance therefore from this thy wickedness: and pray to God, if perhaps this cogitation of thy heart may be remitted thee.
text.NABRE: 8:22 Repent of this wickedness of yours and pray to the Lord that, if possible, your intention may be forgiven.

text.Base: For I see that you are in the gall of bitterness and the chains of wickedness.”
text.KJV: 8:23 For I perceiue that thou art in the gall of bitternesse, and in the bond of iniquitie.
text.DRV: 8:23 For I see thou art in the gall of bitterness and the obligation of iniquity.
text.NABRE: 8:23 For I see that you are filled with bitter gall and are in the bonds of iniquity.”

text.Base: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may happen to me.”
text.KJV: 8:24 Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for mee, that none of these things which ye haue spoken, come vpon me.
text.DRV: 8:24 And Simon answering said: Pray you for me to our Lord, that nothing come upon me of these things which you have said.
text.NABRE: 8:24 Simon said in reply, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may come upon me.”

text.Base: Now after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, proclaiming the good news to many villages of the Samaritans.
text.KJV: 8:25 And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Hierusalem, and preached the Gospel in many villages of the Samaritanes.
text.DRV: 8:25 And they indeed having testified and spoken the word of our Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and evangelized to many countries of the Samaritans.
text.NABRE: 8:25 So when they had testified and proclaimed the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem and preached the good news to many Samaritan villages. Philip and the Ethiopian.

text.Base: Then an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go toward the south to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” (This is a wilderness road.)
text.KJV: 8:26 And the Angel of the Lord spake vnto Philip, saying, Arise, and goe toward the South, vnto the way that goeth downe from Hierusalem vnto Gaza, which is desert.
text.DRV: 8:26 And an Angel of our Lord spake to Philippe, saying: Arise, and go toward the South, to the way that goeth down from Jerusalem into Gaza: this is desert.
text.NABRE: 8:26 Then the angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, “Get up and head south on the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza, the desert route.”

text.Base: So he got up and went. Now there was an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of the Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, in charge of her entire treasury. He had come to Jerusalem to worship
text.KJV: 8:27 And hee arose, and went: and behold, a man of Ethiopia, an Eunuch of great authority vnder Candace queene of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Hierusalem for to worship,
text.DRV: 8:27 And rising he went. And behold, a man of Æthiopia, an Eunuch, of great authority under Candace the queen of the Æthiopians, who was over all her treasures, was come to Jerusalem to adore:
text.NABRE: 8:27 So he got up and set out. Now there was an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of the Candace, that is, the queen of the Ethiopians, in charge of her entire treasury, who had come to Jerusalem to worship,

text.Base: and was returning home; seated in his chariot, he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
text.KJV: 8:28 Was returning, and sitting in his charet, read Esaias the Prophet.
text.DRV: 8:28 and he was returning and sitting upon his chariot, and reading Esay the Prophet.
text.NABRE: 8:28 and was returning home. Seated in his chariot, he was reading the prophet Isaiah.

text.Base: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over to this chariot and join it.”
text.KJV: 8:29 Then the Spirit saide vnto Philip, Goe neere, and ioyne thy selfe to this charet.
text.DRV: 8:29 And the Spirit said to Philippe: Go near, and join thyself to this same chariot.
text.NABRE: 8:29 The Spirit said to Philip, “Go and join up with that chariot.”

text.Base: So Philip ran up to it and heard him reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
text.KJV: 8:30 And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him reade the Prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
text.DRV: 8:30 And Philippe running thereunto, heard him reading Esay the Prophet, and he said: Trowest thou that thou understandest the things which thou readest?
text.NABRE: 8:30 Philip ran up and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”

text.Base: He replied, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to get in and sit beside him.
text.KJV: 8:31 And hee said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip, that hee would come vp, and sit with him.
text.DRV: 8:31 Who said: And how can I, unless some man shew me? & he desired Philippe that he would come up and sit with him.
text.NABRE: 8:31 He replied, “How can I, unless someone instructs me?” So he invited Philip to get in and sit with him.

text.Base: Now the passage of the scripture that he was reading was this: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter, and like a lamb silent before its shearer, so he does not open his mouth.
text.KJV: 8:32 The place of the Scripture, which hee read, was this, Hee was led as a sheepe to the slaughter, & like a Lambe dumbe before the shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
text.DRV: 8:32 And the place of the scripture which he did read, was this: As a sheep to slaughter was he led: and as a lamb before his shearer, without voice, so did he not open his mouth.
text.NABRE: 8:32 This was the scripture passage he was reading: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter, and as a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he opened not his mouth.

text.Base: In his humiliation justice was denied him. Who can describe his generation? For his life is taken from the earth.”
text.KJV: 8:33 In his humiliation, his Iudgement was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? For his life is taken from the earth.
text.DRV: 8:33 In humility his judgement was taken away. His generation who shall declare, for from the earth shall his life be taken?
text.NABRE: 8:33 In (his) humiliation justice was denied him. Who will tell of his posterity? For his life is taken from the earth.”

text.Base: The eunuch asked Philip, “About whom, may I ask you, does the prophet say this, about himself or about someone else?”
text.KJV: 8:34 And the Eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the Prophet this? of himselfe, or of some other man?
text.DRV: 8:34 And the Eunuch answering Philip, said: I beseech thee, of whom doth the Prophet speak this? of himself, or of some other?
text.NABRE: 8:34 Then the eunuch said to Philip in reply, “I beg you, about whom is the prophet saying this? About himself, or about someone else?”

text.Base: Then Philip began to speak, and starting with this scripture, he proclaimed to him the good news about Jesus.
text.KJV: 8:35 Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same Scripture, and preached vnto him Iesus.
text.DRV: 8:35 And, Philip opening his mouth, and beginning from this scripture, evangelized unto him Jesus.
text.NABRE: 8:35 Then Philip opened his mouth and, beginning with this scripture passage, he proclaimed Jesus to him.

text.Base: As they were going along the road, they came to some water; and the eunuch said, “Look, here is water! What is to prevent me from being baptized?”
text.KJV: 8:36 And as they went on their way, they came vnto a certaine water: and the Eunuch said, See, here is water, what doeth hinder me to be baptized?
text.DRV: 8:36 And as they went by the way, they came to a certain water: and the Eunuch said: Lo water, who doth let me to be baptized?
text.NABRE: 8:36 As they traveled along the road they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Look, there is water. What is to prevent my being baptized?”

text.Base: He commanded the chariot to stop, and both of them, Philip and the eunuch, went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
text.KJV: 8:38 And he commanded the charet to stand still: and they went downe both into the water, both Philip, and the Eunuch, and he baptized him.
text.DRV: 8:38 And he commanded the chariot to stay: and both went down into the water, Philip and the Eunuch, and he baptized him.
text.NABRE: 8:38 Then he ordered the chariot to stop, and Philip and the eunuch both went down into the water, and he baptized him.

text.Base: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away; the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
text.KJV: 8:39 And when they were come vp out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the Eunuch saw him no more: and hee went on his way reioycing.
text.DRV: 8:39 And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of our Lord took away Philip, & the Eunuch saw him no more. And he went on his way rejoicing.
text.NABRE: 8:39 When they came out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, but continued on his way rejoicing.

text.Base: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he was passing through the region, he proclaimed the good news to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.
text.KJV: 8:40 But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing thorow he preached in all the cities, till he came to Cesarea.
text.DRV: 8:40 But Philip was found in Azotus, and passing through, he evangelized to all the cities, till he came to Cæsarea.
text.NABRE: 8:40 Philip came to Azotus, and went about proclaiming the good news to all the towns until he reached Caesarea.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest
text.Rotherham: But, Saul, yet breathing threatening and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, going unto the High-priest,
text.KJV: 9:1 And Saul yet breathing out threatnings & slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went vnto the high Priest,
text.DRV: 9:1 And Saul as yet breathing forth threatenings and slaughter against the Disciples of our Lord, came to the high priest,
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 - Saul’s Conversion. Now Saul, still breathing murderous threats against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest

text.NRSV: and asked him for letters to the synagogues at Damascus, so that if he found any who belonged to the Way, men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: asked from him letters for Damascus, unto the synagogues; to the end that, if he should find, any, who were of The Way, whether men or women, he might bring them, bound, unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 9:2 And desired of him letters to Damascus, to the Synagogues, that if hee found any of this way, whether they were men or women, hee might bring them bound vnto Hierusalem.
text.DRV: 9:2 and asked letters of him unto Damascus to the Synagogues that if he had found any men and women of this way, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: 9:2 and asked him for letters to the synagogues in Damascus, that, if he should find any men or women who belonged to the Way, he might bring them back to Jerusalem in chains.

text.NRSV: Now as he was going along and approaching Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.
text.Rotherham: But, as he was journeying, it came to pass that he was drawing near unto Damascus, and, suddenly, there flashed around him a light out of heaven;
text.KJV: 9:3 And as he iourneyed he came neere Damascus, and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heauen.
text.DRV: 9:3 And as he went on his journey, it chanced that he drew nigh to Damascus: and suddenly a light from Heaven shined round about him.
text.NABRE: 9:3 On his journey, as he was nearing Damascus, a light from the sky suddenly flashed around him.

text.NRSV: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?”
text.Rotherham: And, falling unto the earth, he heard a voice saying unto him—Saul! Saul! why, me, art thou persecuting?
text.KJV: 9:4 And he fel to the earth, and heard a voice saying vnto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
text.DRV: 9:4 And falling on the ground, he heard a voice saying to him, Saul, Saul why persecutest thou me?
text.NABRE: 9:4 He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?”

text.NRSV: He asked, “Who are you, Lord?” The reply came, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Who art thou, Lord? And, he, [said] —I, am Jesus, whom, thou, art persecuting!
text.KJV: 9:5 And he said, Who art thou Lord? And the Lord said, I am Iesus whom thou persecutest: It is hard for thee to kicke against the prickes.
text.DRV: 9:5 Who said: Who art thou Lord? And he: I am Jesus whom thou doest persecute. It is hard for thee to kick against the prick.
text.NABRE: 9:5 He said, “Who are you, sir?” The reply came, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.

text.NRSV: But get up and enter the city, and you will be told what you are to do.”
text.Rotherham: But rise up, and enter into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.
text.KJV: 9:6 And he trembling and astonished, said, Lord, what wilt thou haue mee to doe? And the Lord said vnto him, Arise, and goe into the citie, and it shall be told thee what thou must doe.
text.DRV: 9:6 And trembling and being astonished he said: Lord, what wilt thou have me to do?
text.NABRE: 9:6 Now get up and go into the city and you will be told what you must do.”

text.NRSV: The men who were traveling with him stood speechless because they heard the voice but saw no one.
text.Rotherham: But, the men who were accompanying him, stood speechless,—hearing, indeed, the voice, but beholding no one.
text.KJV: 9:7 And the men which iourneyed with him, stood speechlesse, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.
text.DRV: 9:7 And our Lord to him: Arise and go into the city, & it shall be told thee what thou must do. But the men that went in company with him, stood amazed, hearing the voice, but seeing no man.
text.NABRE: 9:7 The men who were traveling with him stood speechless, for they heard the voice but could see no one.

text.NRSV: Saul got up from the ground, and though his eyes were open, he could see nothing; so they led him by the hand and brought him into Damascus.
text.Rotherham: And Saul arose from the earth, and, his eyes being opened, he could see nothing; and, taking him by the hand, they led him into Damascus,—
text.KJV: 9:8 And Saul arose from the earth, and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.
text.DRV: 9:8 And Saul rose up from the ground, and his eyes being opened, he saw nothing. And they drawing him by the hands, brought him into Damascus.
text.NABRE: 9:8 Saul got up from the ground, but when he opened his eyes he could see nothing; so they led him by the hand and brought him to Damascus.

text.NRSV: For three days he was without sight, and neither ate nor drank.
text.Rotherham: And he was three days without seeing, and did neither eat nor drink.
text.KJV: 9:9 And he was three dayes without sight, and neither did eate, nor drinke.
text.DRV: 9:9 And he was there three days not seeing, and he did neither eat nor drink.
text.NABRE: 9:9 For three days he was unable to see, and he neither ate nor drank.

text.NRSV: Now there was a disciple in Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias.” He answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now there was a certain disciple in Damascus, by name Ananias; and, the Lord, said unto him in a vision—Ananias! And, he, said—Lo! I, [am here], Lord!
text.KJV: 9:10 ¶ And there was a certaine disciple at Damascus, named Ananias, and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
text.DRV: 9:10 And there was a certain Disciple at Damascus, named Ananias: and our Lord said to him in a vision, Ananias. But he said, Lo, here I am Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:10 Saul’s Baptism. There was a disciple in Damascus named Ananias, and the Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias.” He answered, “Here I am, Lord.”

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the street called Straight, and at the house of Judas look for a man of Tarsus named Saul. At this moment he is praying,
text.Rotherham: And the Lord [said] unto him—Rise! go into the street which is called Straight, and seek, in the house of Judas, one Saul, by name, of Tarsus. For lo! he is praying,—
text.KJV: 9:11 And the Lord said vnto him, Arise, and goe into the street, which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Iudas, for one called Saul of Tarsus: for behold, he prayeth,
text.DRV: 9:11 And our Lord to him: Arise, and go into the street that is called Straight: and seek in the house of Judas, one named Saul of Tarsus. For behold he prayeth.
text.NABRE: 9:11 The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the street called Straight and ask at the house of Judas for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is there praying,

text.NRSV: and he has seen in a vision a man named Ananias come in and lay his hands on him so that he might regain his sight.”
text.Rotherham: And hath seen a man [in a vision], Ananias by name, coming in and laying on him his hands, to the intent he should see.
text.KJV: 9:12 And hath seene in a vision a man named Ananias, comming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receiue his sight.
text.DRV: 9:12 (And he saw a man named Ananias, coming in and imposing hands upon him for to receive his sight.)
text.NABRE: 9:12 and [in a vision] he has seen a man named Ananias come in and lay [his] hands on him, that he may regain his sight.”

text.NRSV: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has done to your saints in Jerusalem;
text.Rotherham: And Ananias, answered—Lord! I have heard from many concerning this man, how many evil things unto thy saints he hath done in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: 9:13 Then Ananias answered, Lord, I haue heard by many of this man, how much euill hee hath done to thy Saints at Hierusalem:
text.DRV: 9:13 But Ananias answered: Lord, I have heard by many of this man, how much evil he hath done to thy saints in Jerusalem:
text.NABRE: 9:13 But Ananias replied, “Lord, I have heard from many sources about this man, what evil things he has done to your holy ones in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: and here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who invoke your name.”
text.Rotherham: And, here, he hath authority from the High-priests to bind all them that call upon thy name.
text.KJV: 9:14 And here he hath authoritic from the chiefe Priests, to binde all that call on thy Name.
text.DRV: 9:14 and here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that invocate thy name.
text.NABRE: 9:14 And here he has authority from the chief priests to imprison all who call upon your name.”

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to him, “Go, for he is an instrument whom I have chosen to bring my name before Gentiles and kings and before the people of Israel;
text.Rotherham: But the Lord said unto him—Be going thy way; for, a choice vessel unto me, is this man, to bear my name before both [the] nations and kings, and the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 9:15 But the Lord said vnto him, Goe thy way: for hee is a chosen vessell vnto me, to beare my Name before the Gentiles, and Kings, and the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 9:15 And our Lord said to him: Go, for a vessel of election is this man unto me, to carry my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 9:15 But the Lord said to him, “Go, for this man is a chosen instrument of mine to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites,

text.NRSV: I myself will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.”
text.Rotherham: For, I, will let him understand how many things he must needs, for my name, suffer.
text.KJV: 9:16 For I will shew him how great things hee must suffer for my Names sake.
text.DRV: 9:16 For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name.
text.NABRE: 9:16 and I will show him what he will have to suffer for my name.”

text.NRSV: So Ananias went and entered the house. He laid his hands on Saul and said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on your way here, has sent me so that you may regain your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: And Ananias departed, and entered into the house; and, laying upon him his hands, said—Saul, brother! The Lord, hath sent me,—Jesus, who appeared unto thee in the way by which thou wast coming,—that thou mayest recover sight, and be filled with Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 9:17 And Ananias went his way, and entred into the house, and putting his hands on him, said, Brother Saul, the Lord (euen Iesus that appeared vnto thee in the way as thou camest) hath sent me, that thou mightest receiue thy sight, and be filled with the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 9:17 And Ananias went, and entered into the house: and imposing hands upon him, he said: Brother Saul, our Lord Jesus hath sent me, he that appeared to thee in the way that thou camest; that thou mayest see and be filled with the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 9:17 So Ananias went and entered the house; laying his hands on him, he said, “Saul, my brother, the Lord has sent me, Jesus who appeared to you on the way by which you came, that you may regain your sight and be filled with the holy Spirit.”

text.NRSV: And immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and his sight was restored. Then he got up and was baptized,
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, there fell from him—from his eyes,—as it were scales; he recovered sight also, and, arising, was immersed:
text.KJV: 9:18 And immediatly there fell from his eyes as it had bene scales, and he receiued sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.
text.DRV: 9:18 And forthwith there fell from his eyes as it were scales, and he received sight: and rising he was baptized.
text.NABRE: 9:18 Immediately things like scales fell from his eyes and he regained his sight. He got up and was baptized,

text.NRSV: and after taking some food, he regained his strength. For several days he was with the disciples in Damascus,
text.Rotherham: And, receiving food, gained strength. And he came to be with the disciples who were in Damascus certain days;
text.KJV: 9:19 And when hee had receiued meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certaine dayes with the disciples which were at Damascus.
text.DRV: 9:19 And when he had taken meat, he was strengthened. And he was with the Disciples that were at Damascus, for certain days.
text.NABRE: 9:19 and when he had eaten, he recovered his strength. Saul Preaches in Damascus. He stayed some days with the disciples in Damascus,

text.NRSV: and immediately he began to proclaim Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “He is the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, in the synagogues, he began proclaiming Jesus, that, This, is the Son of God.
text.KJV: 9:20 And straightway hee preached Christ in the Synagogues, that hee is the Sonne of God.
text.DRV: 9:20 And incontinent entering into the Synagogues, he preached Jesus, that this is the Son of God.
text.NABRE: 9:20 and he began at once to proclaim Jesus in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.

text.NRSV: All who heard him were amazed and said, “Is not this the man who made havoc in Jerusalem among those who invoked this name? And has he not come here for the purpose of bringing them bound before the chief priests?”
text.Rotherham: And all who were hearing were astonished, and began to say—is not, this, he who destroyed, in Jerusalem, them that invoke this name; and, here, for this purpose, had come, in order that he might lead them, bound, unto the High-priests?
text.KJV: 9:21 But all that heard him, were amazed, and said, Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this Name in Hierusalem, and came hither for that intent that he might bring them bound vnto the chiefe Priests?
text.DRV: 9:21 And all that heard, were astonished, and said: Is not this he that expugned in Jerusalem those that invocated this name; and came hither to this purpose that he might bring them bound to the chief priests?
text.NABRE: 9:21 All who heard him were astounded and said, “Is not this the man who in Jerusalem ravaged those who call upon this name, and came here expressly to take them back in chains to the chief priests?”

text.NRSV: Saul became increasingly more powerful and confounded the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus was the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: But, Saul, was the more gaining power; and was confounding the Jews who dwelt in Damascus, shewing, by comparison, that—This, is the Christ.
text.KJV: 9:22 But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Iewes which dwelt at Damascus, proouing that this is very Christ.
text.DRV: 9:22 But Saul waxed mighty much more and confounded the Jews that dwelt at Damascus, affirming that this is Christ.
text.NABRE: 9:22 But Saul grew all the stronger and confounded [the] Jews who lived in Damascus, proving that this is the Messiah.

text.NRSV: After some time had passed, the Jews plotted to kill him,
text.Rotherham: Now, when a considerable number of days were being fulfilled, the Jews took counsel together to kill him;
text.KJV: 9:23 ¶ And after that many dayes were fulfilled, the Iewes tooke counsel to kill him.
text.DRV: 9:23 And when many days were passed, the Jews consulted that they might kill him.
text.NABRE: 9:23 Saul Visits Jerusalem. After a long time had passed, the Jews conspired to kill him,

text.NRSV: but their plot became known to Saul. They were watching the gates day and night so that they might kill him;
text.Rotherham: But their plot was made known unto Saul,—and they were even narrowly watching the gates, both day and night, that they might kill him;
text.KJV: 9:24 But their laying awaite was knowen of Saul: and they watched the gates day and night to kill him.
text.DRV: 9:24 But their conspiracy came to Saul's knowledge. And they kept the gates also day and night, that they might kill him.
text.NABRE: 9:24 but their plot became known to Saul. Now they were keeping watch on the gates day and night so as to kill him,

text.NRSV: but his disciples took him by night and let him down through an opening in the wall, lowering him in a basket.
text.Rotherham: But the disciples, taking him by night, through the wall, let him down, lowering him in a basket.
text.KJV: 9:25 Then the disciples tooke him by night, and let him downe by the wall in a basket.
text.DRV: 9:25 But the Disciples taking him in the night, conveyed him away by the wall, letting him down in a basket.
text.NABRE: 9:25 but his disciples took him one night and let him down through an opening in the wall, lowering him in a basket.

text.NRSV: When he had come to Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples; and they were all afraid of him, for they did not believe that he was a disciple.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had arrived in Jerusalem, he made attempts to join himself unto the disciples; and all were afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.
text.KJV: 9:26 And when Saul was come to Hierusalem, he assayed to ioyne himselfe to the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, and beleeued not that he was a disciple.
text.DRV: 9:26 And when he was come into Jerusalem, he assayed to join himself to the Disciples, & all feared him, not believing that he was a Disciple.
text.NABRE: 9:26 When he arrived in Jerusalem he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

text.NRSV: But Barnabas took him, brought him to the apostles, and described for them how on the road he had seen the Lord, who had spoken to him, and how at Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: But, Barnabas, taking him, brought him unto the apostles, and related unto them,—how, in the way, he had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken unto him; and how, in Damascus, he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
text.KJV: 9:27 But Barnabas tooke him, and brought him to the Apostles, and declared vnto them how hee had seene the Lord in the way, and that hee had spoken to him, and how hee had preached boldly at Damascus in the Name of Iesus.
text.DRV: 9:27 But Barnabas took him & brought him to the Apostles, and told them how in the way he had seen our Lord, and that he spake unto him, & how in Damascus he dealt confidently in the name of Jesus.
text.NABRE: 9:27 Then Barnabas took charge of him and brought him to the apostles, and he reported to them how on the way he had seen the Lord and that he had spoken to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken out boldly in the name of Jesus.

text.NRSV: So he went in and out among them in Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he was with them, coming in and going out in Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord;
text.KJV: 9:28 And he was with them comming in, and going out at Hierusalem.
text.DRV: 9:28 And he was with them going in & going out in Jerusalem, & dealing confidently in the name of our Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:28 He moved about freely with them in Jerusalem, and spoke out boldly in the name of the Lord.

text.NRSV: He spoke and argued with the Hellenists; but they were attempting to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And was both speaking and discussing with the Grecian Jews,—but, they, were setting to work to kill him.
text.KJV: 9:29 And he spake boldly in the Name of the Lord Iesus, and disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.
text.DRV: 9:29 He spake also to the Gentiles, & disputed with the Greeks: but they sought to kill him.
text.NABRE: 9:29 He also spoke and debated with the Hellenists, but they tried to kill him.

text.NRSV: When the believers learned of it, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
text.Rotherham: And the brethren, discovering it, brought him down into Caesarea, and sent him away unto Tarsus.
text.KJV: 9:30 Which when the brethren knewe, they brought him downe to Cesarea, and sent him foorth to Tarsus.
text.DRV: 9:30 Which when the brethren had known, they brought him down to Cæsarea & sent him away to Tarsus.
text.NABRE: 9:30 And when the brothers learned of this, they took him down to Caesarea and sent him on his way to Tarsus.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace and was built up. Living in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it increased in numbers.
text.Rotherham: So then, the assembly throughout the whole of Judaea and Galilee and Samaria, had peace, building itself up, and going on its way in the fear of the Lord; and, by the advocacy of the Holy Spirit, was being multiplied.
text.KJV: 9:31 Then had the Churches rest thorowout all Iudea, and Galilee, and Samaria, and were edified, and walking in the feare of the Lord, and in the comfort of the holy Ghost, were multiplied.
text.DRV: 9:31 The Church truly through all Jewry & Galilee and Samaria had peace, & was edified, walking in the fear of our Lord, and was replenished with the consolation of the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 9:31 The Church at Peace. The church throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria was at peace. It was being built up and walked in the fear of the Lord, and with the consolation of the holy Spirit it grew in numbers.

text.NRSV: Now as Peter went here and there among all the believers, he came down also to the saints living in Lydda.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Peter, going through all [quarters] went down unto the saints also dwelling in Lydda.
text.KJV: 9:32 ¶ And it came to passe, as Peter passed thorowout all quarters, he came downe also to the Saints, which dwelt at Lydda.
text.DRV: 9:32 And it came to pass, that Peter as he passed through all, came to the saints that dwelt at Lydda.
text.NABRE: 9:32 Peter Heals Aeneas at Lydda. As Peter was passing through every region, he went down to the holy ones living in Lydda.

text.NRSV: There he found a man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years, for he was paralyzed.
text.Rotherham: And he found there a certain man, by name Aeneas, who, for eight years, had been lying prostrate upon a bed, for he was paralysed.
text.KJV: 9:33 And there he found a certaine man named Aeneas, which had kept his bed eight yeeres, and was sicke of the palsie.
text.DRV: 9:33 and he found there a certain man named Æneas, lying in his bed from eight years before, who had the palsy.
text.NABRE: 9:33 There he found a man named Aeneas, who had been confined to bed for eight years, for he was paralyzed.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; get up and make your bed!” And immediately he got up.
text.Rotherham: And Peter said unto him—Aeneas! Jesus Christ healeth thee! Arise, and smooth thy bed for thyself. And, straightway, he arose.
text.KJV: 9:34 And Peter said vnto him, Aeneas, Iesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.
text.DRV: 9:34 And Peter said to him: Æneas, our Lord Jesus Christ heal thee: arise, and make thy bed. And incontinent he arose.
text.NABRE: 9:34 Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and make your bed.” He got up at once.

text.NRSV: And all the residents of Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And all who dwelt in Lydda and Saron, saw him,—and, they, turned unto the Lord.
text.KJV: 9:35 And all that dwelt at Lydda, and Saron, saw him, and turned to the Lord.
text.DRV: 9:35 And all that dwelt at Lydda and Sarona, saw him: who converted to our Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:35 And all the inhabitants of Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Now in Joppa there was a disciple whose name was Tabitha, which in Greek is Dorcas. She was devoted to good works and acts of charity.
text.Rotherham: Now, in Joppa, there was a certain female disciple, by name Tabitha, which, being translated, means Dorcas [a Gazelle] . The same, was full of good works and alms which she was doing.
text.KJV: 9:36 ¶ Now there was at Ioppa a certain disciple, named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: This woman was full of good works, and almes deeds, which she did.
text.DRV: 9:36 And in Joppe there was a certain Disciple named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas. This woman was full of good works & alms-deeds which she did.
text.NABRE: 9:36 Peter Restores Tabitha to Life. Now in Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha (which translated means Dorcas). She was completely occupied with good deeds and almsgiving.

text.NRSV: At that time she became ill and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in a room upstairs.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in those days, that she, sickening, died; and, bathing her, they laid her in an upper room.
text.KJV: 9:37 And it came to passe in those dayes that she was sicke, and died: whome when they had washed, they laid her in an vpper chamber.
text.DRV: 9:37 And it came to pass in those days, that she was sick and died. Whom when they had washed, they laid her in an upper chamber.
text.NABRE: 9:37 Now during those days she fell sick and died, so after washing her, they laid [her] out in a room upstairs.

text.NRSV: Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, who heard that Peter was there, sent two men to him with the request, “Please come to us without delay.”
text.Rotherham: Now, Lydda being nigh unto Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was therein, sent off two men unto him, beseeching him—Do not delay to come through unto us!
text.KJV: 9:38 And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Ioppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent vnto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.
text.DRV: 9:38 And whereas Lydda was nigh to Joppe, the Disciples hearing that Peter was in it, they sent two men unto him, desiring him: Be not loath to come so far as to us.
text.NABRE: 9:38 Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men to him with the request, “Please come to us without delay.”

text.NRSV: So Peter got up and went with them; and when he arrived, they took him to the room upstairs. All the widows stood beside him, weeping and showing tunics and other clothing that Dorcas had made while she was with them.
text.Rotherham: And Peter, arising, went with them,—whom, when he arrived, they brought up into the upper room; and there stood by him all the widows, weeping, and showing the tunics and mantles—whatsoever things Dorcas was making while she was with them.
text.KJV: 9:39 Then Peter arose and went with them: when he was come, they brought him into the vpper chamber: And all the widowes stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while shee was with them.
text.DRV: 9:39 And Peter rising up came with them. And when he was come, they brought him into the upper chamber: and all the widows stood about him weeping, and shewing him the coats and garments which Dorcas made them.
text.NABRE: 9:39 So Peter got up and went with them. When he arrived, they took him to the room upstairs where all the widows came to him weeping and showing him the tunics and cloaks that Dorcas had made while she was with them.

text.NRSV: Peter put all of them outside, and then he knelt down and prayed. He turned to the body and said, “Tabitha, get up.” Then she opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, she sat up.
text.Rotherham: But Peter, putting them all outside, knelt down and prayed; and, turning towards the body, said—Tabitha, arise! And she, opening her eyes and seeing Peter, sat up.
text.KJV: 9:40 But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled downe, and prayed, and turning him to the body, said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sate vp.
text.DRV: 9:40 And all being put forth, Peter falling on his knees prayed, and turning to the body he said: Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes: and seeing Peter, she sat up.
text.NABRE: 9:40 Peter sent them all out and knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to her body and said, “Tabitha, rise up.” She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.

text.NRSV: He gave her his hand and helped her up. Then calling the saints and widows, he showed her to be alive.
text.Rotherham: And, giving her his hand, he raised her up; and, calling the saints and the widows, presented her, living.
text.KJV: 9:41 And he gaue her his hand, and lift her vp: and when hee had called the Saints & widowes, presented her aliue.
text.DRV: 9:41 And giving her his hand, he lifted her up. And when he had called the saints and the widows, he presented her alive.
text.NABRE: 9:41 He gave her his hand and raised her up, and when he had called the holy ones and the widows, he presented her alive.

text.NRSV: This became known throughout Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And it became known throughout the whole of Joppa, and many believed upon the Lord.
text.KJV: 9:42 And it was knowen thorowout all Ioppa, and many beleeued in the Lord.
text.DRV: 9:42 And it was made known throughout all Joppe: and many believed in our Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:42 This became known all over Joppa, and many came to believe in the Lord.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile he stayed in Joppa for some time with a certain Simon, a tanner.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, for a considerable number of days, he abode in Joppa, with one Simon, a tanner.
text.KJV: 9:43 And it came to passe, that he taried many dayes in Ioppa, with one Simon a Tanner.
text.DRV: 9:43 And it came to pass that he abode many days in Joppe, with one Simon a tanner.
text.NABRE: 9:43 And he stayed a long time in Joppa with Simon, a tanner.

text.NRSV: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of the Italian Cohort, as it was called.
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man in Caesarea, by name Cornelius, a centurion of the band called Italian,—
text.KJV: 10:1 There was a certaine man in Cesarea, called Cornelius, a Centurion of þ band called the Italian band,
text.DRV: 10:1 And there was a certain man in Cæsarea, named Cornelius, Centurion of that which is called the Italian band,
text.NABRE: 10:1 IV. The Inauguration of the Gentile Mission Chapter 10 - The Vision of Cornelius. Now in Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of the Cohort called the Italica,

text.NRSV: He was a devout man who feared God with all his household; he gave alms generously to the people and prayed constantly to God.
text.Rotherham: Devout, and fearing God with all his house, doing many alms unto the people, and supplicating God continually,
text.KJV: 10:2 A deuout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gaue much almes to the people, and prayed to God alway.
text.DRV: 10:2 religious, & fearing God with all his house, doing many alms-deeds to the people. And always praying to God,
text.NABRE: 10:2 devout and God-fearing along with his whole household, who used to give alms generously to the Jewish people and pray to God constantly.

text.NRSV: One afternoon at about three o’clock he had a vision in which he clearly saw an angel of God coming in and saying to him, “Cornelius.”
text.Rotherham: Saw, in a vision, manifestly, as if about the ninth hour of the day, a messenger of God, coming in unto him, and saying unto him—Cornelius!
text.KJV: 10:3 He saw in a vision euidently, about the ninth houre of the day, an Angel of God comming in to him, and saying vnto him Cornelius.
text.DRV: 10:3 he saw in a vision manifestly, about the ninth hour of the day, an Angel of God coming in unto him and saying to him: Cornelius.
text.NABRE: 10:3 One afternoon about three o’clock, he saw plainly in a vision an angel of God come in to him and say to him, “Cornelius.”

text.NRSV: He stared at him in terror and said, “What is it, Lord?” He answered, “Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
text.Rotherham: And he, looking steadfastly at him, and becoming full of fear, said—What is it, Lord? And he said unto him—Thy prayers and thine alms, have gone up for a memorial before God.
text.KJV: 10:4 And when he looked on him, hee was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said vnto him, Thy praiers and thine almes are come vp for a memorial before God.
text.DRV: 10:4 But he beholding him, taken with fear, said: Who art thou Lord? And he said to him: Thy prayers and thy alms-deeds are ascended into remembrance in the sight of God.
text.NABRE: 10:4 He looked intently at him and, seized with fear, said, “What is it, sir?” He said to him, “Your prayers and almsgiving have ascended as a memorial offering before God.

text.NRSV: Now send men to Joppa for a certain Simon who is called Peter;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, send men unto Joppa, and fetch one Simon who is surnamed Peter,—
text.KJV: 10:5 And now send men to Ioppa, and call for one Simon, whose sirname is Peter.
text.DRV: 10:5 And now send men unto Joppe, and call hither one Simon that is surnamed Peter.
text.NABRE: 10:5 Now send some men to Joppa and summon one Simon who is called Peter.

text.NRSV: he is lodging with Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the seaside.”
text.Rotherham: The same is a guest with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea.
text.KJV: 10:6 Hee lodgeth with one Simon a Tanner, whose house is by the Sea side; he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to doe.
text.DRV: 10:6 He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side. He will tell thee what thou must do.
text.NABRE: 10:6 He is staying with another Simon, a tanner, who has a house by the sea.”

text.NRSV: When the angel who spoke to him had left, he called two of his slaves and a devout soldier from the ranks of those who served him,
text.Rotherham: And, when the messenger who had been speaking with him had departed, calling two of the domestics, and a devout soldier of them that constantly attended him,
text.KJV: 10:7 And when the Angel which spake vnto Cornelius, was departed, he called two of his houshold seruants, and a deuout souldier of them that waited on him continually.
text.DRV: 10:7 And when the Angel was departed that spake to him, he called two of his household, and a soldier that feared our Lord, of them that were under him.
text.NABRE: 10:7 When the angel who spoke to him had left, he called two of his servants and a devout soldier from his staff,

text.NRSV: and after telling them everything, he sent them to Joppa.
text.Rotherham: And relating everything unto them, he sent them off unto Joppa.
text.KJV: 10:8 And when he had declared all these things vnto them, he sent them to Ioppa.
text.DRV: 10:8 To whom when he had told all, he sent them unto Joppe.
text.NABRE: 10:8 explained everything to them, and sent them to Joppa.

text.NRSV: About noon the next day, as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray.
text.Rotherham: Now, on the morrow, as those men were journeying, and, unto the city, drawing near, Peter went up on the housetop to pray, about the sixth hour;
text.KJV: 10:9 ¶ On the morrow as they went on their iourney, and drew nigh vnto the citie, Peter went vp vpon the house to pray, about the sixth houre.
text.DRV: 10:9 And the next day whiles they were going on their journey, and drawing nigh to the city, Peter went up into the higher parts, to pray about the sixth hour.
text.NABRE: 10:9 The Vision of Peter. The next day, while they were on their way and nearing the city, Peter went up to the roof terrace to pray at about noontime.

text.NRSV: He became hungry and wanted something to eat; and while it was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
text.Rotherham: But he became hungry, and wished to eat,—and, while they were making ready, there came upon him a trance;
text.KJV: 10:10 And he became very hungry, and would haue eaten: But while they made ready, he fell into a traunce,
text.DRV: 10:10 And being hungry, he was desirous to take somewhat. And as they were preparing, there fell upon him an excess of mind:
text.NABRE: 10:10 He was hungry and wished to eat, and while they were making preparations he fell into a trance.

text.NRSV: He saw the heaven opened and something like a large sheet coming down, being lowered to the ground by its four corners.
text.Rotherham: And he beholdeth heaven opened, and, corning down, a kind of vessel, like a large linen cloth, by its four corners, being let down upon the earth,
text.KJV: 10:11 And saw heauen opened, and a certaine vessell descending vnto him, as it had beene a great sheete, knit at the foure corners, and let downe to the earth:
text.DRV: 10:11 and he saw the Heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending, as it were a great linen sheet with four corners let down from Heaven to the earth,
text.NABRE: 10:11 He saw heaven opened and something resembling a large sheet coming down, lowered to the ground by its four corners.

text.NRSV: In it were all kinds of four-footed creatures and reptiles and birds of the air.
text.Rotherham: In which were all the quadrupeds and creeping things of earth and birds of heaven.
text.KJV: 10:12 Wherein were all maner of foure footed beasts of the earth, and wilde beasts, and creeping things, and foules of the ayre.
text.DRV: 10:12 wherein were all four-footed beasts, and that creep on the earth, and fowls of the air.
text.NABRE: 10:12 In it were all the earth’s four-legged animals and reptiles and the birds of the sky.

text.NRSV: Then he heard a voice saying, “Get up, Peter; kill and eat.”
text.Rotherham: And there came a voice unto him—Rise, Peter! slay and eat.
text.KJV: 10:13 And there came a voyce to him, Rise, Peter: kill, and eate.
text.DRV: 10:13 And there came a voice to him: Arise Peter, kill, and eat.
text.NABRE: 10:13 A voice said to him, “Get up, Peter. Slaughter and eat.”

text.NRSV: But Peter said, “By no means, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is profane or unclean.”
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—By no means, Lord! because, at no time, have I eaten anything common or unclean.
text.KJV: 10:14 But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I haue neuer eaten any thing that is common or vncleane.
text.DRV: 10:14 But Peter said: God forbid, Lord; for I did never eat any common and unclean thing.
text.NABRE: 10:14 But Peter said, “Certainly not, sir. For never have I eaten anything profane and unclean.”

text.NRSV: The voice said to him again, a second time, “What God has made clean, you must not call profane.”
text.Rotherham: And a voice [came] again, a second time, unto him—What things, God, hath cleansed, be not, thou, making common.
text.KJV: 10:15 And the voice spake vnto him againe the second time, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
text.DRV: 10:15 And a voice came to him again the second time: That which God hath purified, do not thou call common.
text.NABRE: 10:15 The voice spoke to him again, a second time, “What God has made clean, you are not to call profane.”

text.NRSV: This happened three times, and the thing was suddenly taken up to heaven.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, took place thrice; and, straightway, was the vessel taken up into heaven.
text.KJV: 10:16 This was done thrise: & the vessel was receiued vp againe into heauen.
text.DRV: 10:16 And this was done thrice, & forthwith the vessel was taken up again into Heaven.
text.NABRE: 10:16 This happened three times, and then the object was taken up into the sky.

text.NRSV: Now while Peter was greatly puzzled about what to make of the vision that he had seen, suddenly the men sent by Cornelius appeared. They were asking for Simon’s house and were standing by the gate.
