text.KJV: 16:26 And suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken: and immediately all the doores were opened, and euery ones bands were loosed.
text.DRV: 16:26 But suddenly there was made a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken. And forthwith all the doors were opened: and the bands of all were loosed.
text.NABRE: 16:26 there was suddenly such a severe earthquake that the foundations of the jail shook; all the doors flew open, and the chains of all were pulled loose.

text.NRSV: When the jailer woke up and saw the prison doors wide open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, since he supposed that the prisoners had escaped.
text.Rotherham: And, the prison-keeper, being wakened and seeing that the doors of the prison had been opened, drawing his sword, was about to kill himself, supposing the prisoners to have fled.
text.KJV: 16:27 And the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleepe, and seeing the prison doores open, he drew out his sword, and would haue killed himselfe, supposing that the prisoners had beene fled.
text.DRV: 16:27 And the keeper of the prison waked out of his sleep, and seeing the doors of the prison opened, drawing out his sword, would have killed himself, supposing that the prisoners had been fled.
text.NABRE: 16:27 When the jailer woke up and saw the prison doors wide open, he drew [his] sword and was about to kill himself, thinking that the prisoners had escaped.

text.NRSV: But Paul shouted in a loud voice, “Do not harm yourself, for we are all here.”
text.Rotherham: But Paul called out with a loud voice, saying—By no means, do thyself harm, for we are, one and all, here.
text.KJV: 16:28 But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, Doe thy selfe no harme, for we are all heere.
text.DRV: 16:28 But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying: Do thyself no harm, for we are all here.
text.NABRE: 16:28 But Paul shouted out in a loud voice, “Do no harm to yourself; we are all here.”

text.NRSV: The jailer called for lights, and rushing in, he fell down trembling before Paul and Silas.
text.Rotherham: And, asking for a light, he sprang in, and becoming, agitated, fell down unto Paul and Silas,
text.KJV: 16:29 Then hee called for a light, and sprang in, and came trembling, and fell downe before Paul and Silas,
text.DRV: 16:29 And calling for light, he went in, and trembling fell down to Paul and Silas at their feet:
text.NABRE: 16:29 He asked for a light and rushed in and, trembling with fear, he fell down before Paul and Silas.

text.NRSV: Then he brought them outside and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”
text.Rotherham: and, leading them forth outside, said—Sirs! what must I be doing, that I may be saved?
text.KJV: 16:30 And brought them out, and said, Sirs, what must I doe to be saued?
text.DRV: 16:30 and bringing them forth, he said: Masters, what must I do that I may be saved?
text.NABRE: 16:30 Then he brought them out and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”

text.NRSV: They answered, “Believe on the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Believe on the Lord Jesus, and thou shalt be saved, thou, and thy house.
text.KJV: 16:31 And they saide, Beleeue on the Lord Iesus Christ, and thou shalt be saued, and thy house.
text.DRV: 16:31 But they said: Believe in our Lord Jesus; and thou shalt be saved and thy house.
text.NABRE: 16:31 And they said, “Believe in the Lord Jesus and you and your household will be saved.”

text.NRSV: They spoke the word of the Lord to him and to all who were in his house.
text.Rotherham: And they spake unto him the word of God, with all who were in his house.
text.KJV: 16:32 And they spake vnto him the word of the Lord, and to all that were in his house.
text.DRV: 16:32 And they preached the word of our Lord to him with all that were in his house.
text.NABRE: 16:32 So they spoke the word of the Lord to him and to everyone in his house.

text.NRSV: At the same hour of the night he took them and washed their wounds; then he and his entire household were baptized without delay.
text.Rotherham: And, taking them with him, in that hour of the night, he bathed them from their stripes, and was immersed, he, and his, one and all, on the spot;
text.KJV: 16:33 And hee tooke them the same houre of the night, and washed their stripes, and was baptized, hee and all his, straightway.
text.DRV: 16:33 And he taking them in the same hour of the night, washed their wounds: and himself was baptized and all his house incontinent.
text.NABRE: 16:33 He took them in at that hour of the night and bathed their wounds; then he and all his family were baptized at once.

text.NRSV: He brought them up into the house and set food before them; and he and his entire household rejoiced that he had become a believer in God.
text.Rotherham: and, leading them up into his house, he set near a table,—and exulted, having, with all his house, believed in God.
text.KJV: 16:34 And when he had brought them into his house, hee set meat before them, and reioyced, beleeuing in God with all his house.
text.DRV: 16:34 And when he had brought them into his own house, he laid the table for them, and rejoiced with all his house, believing God.
text.NABRE: 16:34 He brought them up into his house and provided a meal and with his household rejoiced at having come to faith in God.

text.NRSV: When morning came, the magistrates sent the police, saying, “Let those men go.”
text.Rotherham: And. when day came. the magistrates sent off the constables saying—Let those men go!
text.KJV: 16:35 And when it was day, the Magistrates sent the Sergeants, saying, Let those men goe.
text.DRV: 16:35 And when day was come, the magistrates sent the sergeants, saying: Let those men go.
text.NABRE: 16:35 But when it was day, the magistrates sent the lictors with the order, “Release those men.”

text.NRSV: And the jailer reported the message to Paul, saying, “The magistrates sent word to let you go; therefore come out now and go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: And the prison-keeper reported the words unto Paul—The magistrates have sent, that ye be let go. Now, therefore, going forth, be taking your journey in peace.
text.KJV: 16:36 And the keeper of the prison told this saying to Paul, The Magistrates haue sent to let you goe: Now therefore depart, and goe in peace.
text.DRV: 16:36 And the keeper of the prison told these words to Paul, That the magistrates have sent that you should be let go. Now therefore departing, go ye in peace.
text.NABRE: 16:36 The jailer reported the[se] words to Paul, “The magistrates have sent orders that you be released. Now, then, come out and go in peace.”

text.NRSV: But Paul replied, “They have beaten us in public, uncondemned, men who are Roman citizens, and have thrown us into prison; and now are they going to discharge us in secret? Certainly not! Let them come themselves and take us out.”
text.Rotherham: But, Paul, said unto them—Beating us, in public, uncondemned, men that are Romans, they thrust us into prison;—and, now, by stealth, are they thrusting us forth? Nay, verily! but let them come, themselves, and lead us out!
text.KJV: 16:37 But Paul said vnto them, They haue beaten vs openly vncondemned, being Romanes, and haue cast vs into prison, and now doe they thrust vs out priuily? Nay verily, but let them come themselues, and fetch vs out.
text.DRV: 16:37 But Paul said to them: Being whipped openly, uncondemned, men that are Romans, they have cast us into prison: & now do they send us out secretly? Not so, but let them come, & let us out themselves.
text.NABRE: 16:37 But Paul said to them, “They have beaten us publicly, even though we are Roman citizens and have not been tried, and have thrown us into prison. And now, are they going to release us secretly? By no means. Let them come themselves and lead us out.”

text.NRSV: The police reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Roman citizens;
text.Rotherham: And the constables reported unto the magistrates these words; and they were struck with fear, when they heard that they were, Romans;
text.KJV: 16:38 And the Sergeants tolde these words vnto the Magistrates: and they feared when they heard that they were Romanes.
text.DRV: 16:38 And the sergeants reported these words to the magistrates. And they were afraid hearing that they were Romans:
text.NABRE: 16:38 The lictors reported these words to the magistrates, and they became alarmed when they heard that they were Roman citizens.

text.NRSV: so they came and apologized to them. And they took them out and asked them to leave the city.
text.Rotherham: and came, and besought them, and, leading them out, went on to request them to depart from the city.
text.KJV: 16:39 And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the citie.
text.DRV: 16:39 and coming they besought them, & bringing them forth they desired them to depart out of the city.
text.NABRE: 16:39 So they came and placated them, and led them out and asked that they leave the city.

text.NRSV: After leaving the prison they went to Lydia’s home; and when they had seen and encouraged the brothers and sisters there, they departed.
text.Rotherham: And so, coming forth from the prison, they went unto Lydia, and, seeing the brethren, they comforted them, and went forth.
text.KJV: 16:40 And they went out of the prison, and entred into the house of Lydia, and when they had seene the brethren, they comforted them, and departed.
text.DRV: 16:40 And going out of the prison, they entered in unto Lydia: and having seen the brethren, they comforted them, and departed.
text.NABRE: 16:40 When they had come out of the prison, they went to Lydia’s house where they saw and encouraged the brothers, and then they left.

text.NRSV: After they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: And, travelling through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Jews;
text.KJV: 17:1 Now when they had passed thorow Amphipolis, and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Iewes.
text.DRV: 17:1 And when they had walked through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Synagogue of the Jews.
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 - Paul in Thessalonica. When they took the road through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they reached Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.

text.NRSV: And Paul went in, as was his custom, and on three sabbath days he argued with them from the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: and, according to Paul’s custom, he went in unto them, and, for three sabbaths, reasoned with them from the Scriptures,—
text.KJV: 17:2 And Paul, as his maner was, went in vnto them, and three Sabbath dayes reasoned with them out of the Scriptures,
text.DRV: 17:2 And Paul according to his custom entered in unto them, & three Sabboths he discoursed to them out of the Scriptures,
text.NABRE: 17:2 Following his usual custom, Paul joined them, and for three sabbaths he entered into discussions with them from the scriptures,

text.NRSV: explaining and proving that it was necessary for the Messiah to suffer and to rise from the dead, and saying, “This is the Messiah, Jesus whom I am proclaiming to you.”
text.Rotherham: opening up, and setting forth, that it was needful for, the Christ, to suffer, and to arise from among the dead; and [saying], This, is the Christ,—Jesus, whom, I, am declaring unto you.
text.KJV: 17:3 Opening and alleadging, that Christ must needs haue suffered and risen againe from the dead: and that this Iesus whom I preach vnto you, is Christ.
text.DRV: 17:3 declaring and insinuating that it behoved Christ to suffer and to rise again from the dead: & that this is Jesus Christ, whom I preach to you.
text.NABRE: 17:3 expounding and demonstrating that the Messiah had to suffer and rise from the dead, and that “This is the Messiah, Jesus, whom I proclaim to you.”

text.NRSV: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, as did a great many of the devout Greeks and not a few of the leading women.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were persuaded, and cast in their lot with Paul and Silas; also, of the devout Greeks, a great throng, and, of the chief women, not a few.
text.KJV: 17:4 And some of them beleeued, and consorted with Paul and Silas: and of the deuout Greekes a great multitude, and of the chiefe women not a few.
text.DRV: 17:4 And certain of them believed, and were joined to Paul and Silas, and of the Gentiles that served God a great multitude, and noble women not a few.
text.NABRE: 17:4 Some of them were convinced and joined Paul and Silas; so, too, a great number of Greeks who were worshipers, and not a few of the prominent women.

text.NRSV: But the Jews became jealous, and with the help of some ruffians in the marketplaces they formed a mob and set the city in an uproar. While they were searching for Paul and Silas to bring them out to the assembly, they attacked Jason’s house.
text.Rotherham: But the Jews, being, jealous, and taking unto themselves certain wicked men, of the rabble, and making a riot, were setting the city in an uproar; and, besieging the house of Jason, were seeking to lead them forth unto the populace,—
text.KJV: 17:5 ¶ But the Iewes which beleeued not, mooued with enuie, tooke vnto them certaine lewd fellowes of the baser sort, and gathered a company, and set all the citie on an vprore, and assaulted the house of Iason, and sought to bring them out to the people.
text.DRV: 17:5 But the Jews envying, & taking unto them of the rascal sort certain naughty men, and making a tumult, stirred the city: and besetting Jason's house, sought to bring them forth unto the people.
text.NABRE: 17:5 But the Jews became jealous and recruited some worthless men loitering in the public square, formed a mob, and set the city in turmoil. They marched on the house of Jason, intending to bring them before the people’s assembly.

text.NRSV: When they could not find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city authorities, shouting, “These people who have been turning the world upside down have come here also,
text.Rotherham: and, not finding them, they began dragging Jason and certain brethren unto the city-rulers, shouting—They who have thrown the inhabited earth into confusion, the same, hither also, are come,—
text.KJV: 17:6 And when they found them not, they drew Iason, and certaine brethren vnto the rulers of the citie, crying, These that haue turned the world vpside downe, are come hither also,
text.DRV: 17:6 And not finding them, they drew Jason and certain brethren to the princes of the city, crying, That these are they that stir up the world, and are come hither,
text.NABRE: 17:6 When they could not find them, they dragged Jason and some of the brothers before the city magistrates, shouting, “These people who have been creating a disturbance all over the world have now come here,

text.NRSV: and Jason has entertained them as guests. They are all acting contrary to the decrees of the emperor, saying that there is another king named Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: unto whom Jason hath given welcome; and, these all, contrary to the decrees of Caesar, are acting,—saying that there is another king, Jesus.
text.KJV: 17:7 Whom Iason hath receiued: and these all doe contrary to the decrees of Cesar, saying, that there is another King, one Iesus.
text.DRV: 17:7 whom Jason hath received, and all these do against the decrees of Cæsar, saying that there is another King, Jesus.
text.NABRE: 17:7 and Jason has welcomed them. They all act in opposition to the decrees of Caesar and claim instead that there is another king, Jesus.”

text.NRSV: The people and the city officials were disturbed when they heard this,
text.Rotherham: And they troubled the multitude and the city-rulers, when they heard these things;
text.KJV: 17:8 And they troubled the people, and the rulers of the citie, when they heard these things.
text.DRV: 17:8 And they moved the people, and the princes of the city hearing these things.
text.NABRE: 17:8 They stirred up the crowd and the city magistrates who, upon hearing these charges,

text.NRSV: and after they had taken bail from Jason and the others, they let them go.
text.Rotherham: and, taking security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.
text.KJV: 17:9 And when they had taken securitie of Iason, and of the other, they let them goe.
text.DRV: 17:9 And taking a satisfaction of Jason and of the rest, they dismissed them.
text.NABRE: 17:9 took a surety payment from Jason and the others before releasing them.

text.NRSV: That very night the believers sent Paul and Silas off to Beroea; and when they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
text.Rotherham: But, the brethren, straightway, during the night, sent away both Paul and Silas unto Beroea, who, indeed, arriving, unto the synagogue of the Jews, went off;
text.KJV: 17:10 ¶ And the brethren immediatly sent away Paul and Silas by night vnto Berea: who comming thither, went into the Synagogue of the Iewes.
text.DRV: 17:10 But the brethren forthwith by night sent away Paul and Silas unto Beroea. Who when they were come, entered into the Synagogue of the Jews.
text.NABRE: 17:10 Paul in Beroea. The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas to Beroea during the night. Upon arrival they went to the synagogue of the Jews.

text.NRSV: These Jews were more receptive than those in Thessalonica, for they welcomed the message with very great eagerness and examined the scriptures every day to see whether these things were so.
text.Rotherham: and, these, were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they welcomed the word with all readiness of mind, daily, searching the Scriptures,—whether these things could be so.
text.KJV: 17:11 These were more noble then those in Thessalonica, in that they receiued the word with all readinesse of minde, and searched the Scriptures dayly, whether those things were so.
text.DRV: 17:11 (And these were more noble than they that are at Thessalonica, who received the word with all greediness, daily searching the scriptures, if these things were so.
text.NABRE: 17:11 These Jews were more fair-minded than those in Thessalonica, for they received the word with all willingness and examined the scriptures daily to determine whether these things were so.

text.NRSV: Many of them therefore believed, including not a few Greek women and men of high standing.
text.Rotherham: Many, therefore, from among them, believed, and, of the Grecian women of the higher class, and of men, not a few.
text.KJV: 17:12 Therefore many of them beleeued: also of honourable women which were Greekes, and of men not a few.
text.DRV: 17:12 And many surely of them believed, and of honest women Gentiles, and men not a few.)
text.NABRE: 17:12 Many of them became believers, as did not a few of the influential Greek women and men.

text.NRSV: But when the Jews of Thessalonica learned that the word of God had been proclaimed by Paul in Beroea as well, they came there too, to stir up and incite the crowds.
text.Rotherham: But, when the Jews from Thessalonica came to know that, in Beroea also, had the word of God been declared by Paul, they came thither also, stirring up and troubling the multitudes.
text.KJV: 17:13 But when the Iewes of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was preached of Paul at Berea, they came thither also, and stirred vp the people.
text.DRV: 17:13 And when the Jews in Thessalonica understood, that at Beroea also the word of God was preached by Paul, they came thither also, moving and troubling the multitude.
text.NABRE: 17:13 But when the Jews of Thessalonica learned that the word of God had now been proclaimed by Paul in Beroea also, they came there too to cause a commotion and stir up the crowds.

text.NRSV: Then the believers immediately sent Paul away to the coast, but Silas and Timothy remained behind.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, then, immediately, the brethren sent away, Paul, to be journeying as far as unto the sea; and both Silas and Timothy stayed behind, there.
text.KJV: 17:14 And then immediatly the brethren sent away Paul, to goe as it were to the sea: but Silas and Timotheus abode there still.
text.DRV: 17:14 And then immediately the brethren sent away Paul, to go unto the sea: but Silas and Timothee remained there.
text.NABRE: 17:14 So the brothers at once sent Paul on his way to the seacoast, while Silas and Timothy remained behind.

text.NRSV: Those who conducted Paul brought him as far as Athens; and after receiving instructions to have Silas and Timothy join him as soon as possible, they left him.
text.Rotherham: But, they who were conducting Paul, brought him as far as Athens, and, receiving a commandment unto Silas and Timothy, that with, all possible speed, they would come unto him, they departed.
text.KJV: 17:15 And they that conducted Paul, brought him vnto Athens, and receiuing a commaundement vnto Silas and Timotheus, for to come to him with all speed, they departed.
text.DRV: 17:15 And they that conducted Paul, brought him as far as Athens, and receiving commandment of him to Silas and Timothee, that they should come to him very speedily, they departed.
text.NABRE: 17:15 After Paul’s escorts had taken him to Athens, they came away with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens.

text.NRSV: While Paul was waiting for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to see that the city was full of idols.
text.Rotherham: But, while, in Athens, Paul was expecting them, his spirit within him was being urged on, seeing how the city was given to idols.
text.KJV: 17:16 ¶ Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was stirred in him, when hee saw the city wholy giuen to idolatrie.
text.DRV: 17:16 And when Paul expected them at Athens, his spirit was incensed within him, seeing the city given to idolatry.
text.NABRE: 17:16 While Paul was waiting for them in Athens, he grew exasperated at the sight of the city full of idols.

text.NRSV: So he argued in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and also in the marketplace every day with those who happened to be there.
text.Rotherham: So then, he began reasoning in the synagogue with the Jews, and with them who worshipped; and, in the market-place, every day, with them who happened to be at hand.
text.KJV: 17:17 Therefore disputed he in the Synagogue with the Iewes, and with the deuout persons, and in the market dayly with them that met with him.
text.DRV: 17:17 He disputed therefore in the Synagogue with the Jews, & them that served God, and in the market-place, every day with them that were there.
text.NABRE: 17:17 So he debated in the synagogue with the Jews and with the worshipers, and daily in the public square with whoever happened to be there.

text.NRSV: Also some Epicurean and Stoic philosophers debated with him. Some said, “What does this babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign deities.” (This was because he was telling the good news about Jesus and the resurrection.)
text.Rotherham: But, certain both of the Epicurean and of the Stoic philosophers, were encountering him; and some were saying—What might this picker-up-of-scraps wish to be saying? And, others—Of foreign demons, he seemeth to be a declarer: because, of Jesus and the Resurrection, he was announcing the joyful tidings.
text.KJV: 17:18 Then certaine Philosophers of the Epicureans, and of the Stoikes, encountred him: and some said, What will this babbler say? Othersome, He seemeth to be a setter foorth of strange gods: because hee preached vnto them Iesus, and the resurrection.
text.DRV: 17:18 And certain Philosophers of the Epicures and the Stoics disputed with him, and certain said, what is it that this word-sower would say? But others: He seemeth to be a preacher of new gods: because he preached to them Jesus and the resurrection.
text.NABRE: 17:18 Even some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion. Some asked, “What is this scavenger trying to say?” Others said, “He sounds like a promoter of foreign deities,” because he was preaching about ‘Jesus’ and ‘Resurrection.’

text.NRSV: So they took him and brought him to the Areopagus and asked, “May we know what this new teaching is that you are presenting?
text.Rotherham: And so, laying hold of him, they brought him up, to the Hill of Mars, saying—Can we get to know what this new teaching is, which, by thee, is being spoken.
text.KJV: 17:19 And they tooke him, and brought him vnto Areopagus, saying, May we know what this new doctrine, whereof thou speakest, is?
text.DRV: 17:19 And apprehending him, they led him to Areopagus, saying: May we know what this new doctrine is that thou speakest of?
text.NABRE: 17:19 They took him and led him to the Areopagus and said, “May we learn what this new teaching is that you speak of?

text.NRSV: It sounds rather strange to us, and we would like to know what it means.”
text.Rotherham: For, certain foreign things, art thou bringing into our hearing: We are minded to get to know, therefore, what these things please to be!
text.KJV: 17:20 For thou bringest certaine strange things to our eares: we would know therefore what these things meane.
text.DRV: 17:20 for thou bringest in certain new things to our ears. We will know therefore what these things may mean.
text.NABRE: 17:20 For you bring some strange notions to our ears; we should like to know what these things mean.”

text.NRSV: Now all the Athenians and the foreigners living there would spend their time in nothing but telling or hearing something new.
text.Rotherham: Now, all Athenians and the sojourning foreigners, unto nothing else, were devoting their leisure, than to be telling or hearing, something newer.
text.KJV: 17:21 (For all the Athenians and strangers which were there, spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to heare some new thing.)
text.DRV: 17:21 (And all the Athenians, and the strangers sojourning there, employed themselves to nothing else but either to speak, or to hear some news.)
text.NABRE: 17:21 Now all the Athenians as well as the foreigners residing there used their time for nothing else but telling or hearing something new.

text.NRSV: Then Paul stood in front of the Areopagus and said, “Athenians, I see how extremely religious you are in every way.
text.Rotherham: And Paul taking his stand in the midst of the Hill of Mars, said—Ye men of Athens! In every way, how unusually reverent of the demons ye are, I perceive.
text.KJV: 17:22 ¶ Then Paul stood in the mids of Mars-hill, and said, Yee men of Athens, I perceiue that in all things yee are too superstitious.
text.DRV: 17:22 But Paul standing in the midst of Areopagus, said: Ye men of Athens, in all things I perceive you as it were superstitious.
text.NABRE: 17:22 Paul’s Speech at the Areopagus. Then Paul stood up at the Areopagus and said: “You Athenians, I see that in every respect you are very religious.

text.NRSV: For as I went through the city and looked carefully at the objects of your worship, I found among them an altar with the inscription, ‘To an unknown god.’ What therefore you worship as unknown, this I proclaim to you.
text.Rotherham: For, passing through, and carefully observing your objects of devotion, I found an altar also, in which was inscribed—Unto an Unknown God. What, therefore, not knowing, ye reverence, the same, do, I, declare unto you.
text.KJV: 17:23 For as I passed by, and beheld your deuotions, I found an Altar with this inscription, TO THE VNKNOWEN GOD. Whom therefore yee ignorantly worship, him declare I vnto you.
text.DRV: 17:23 For passing by and seeing your idols, I found an altar also where upon was written: To the unknown God. That therefore which you worship, not knowing it, the same do I preach to you.
text.NABRE: 17:23 For as I walked around looking carefully at your shrines, I even discovered an altar inscribed, ‘To an Unknown God.’ What therefore you unknowingly worship, I proclaim to you.

text.NRSV: The God who made the world and everything in it, he who is Lord of heaven and earth, does not live in shrines made by human hands,
text.Rotherham: The God that made the world and all things that are therein, the same, being, Lord, of heaven and earth, not in hand-made shrines, doth dwell,
text.KJV: 17:24 God that made the world, and all things therein, seeing that hee is Lord of heauen and earth, dwelleth not in Temples made with hands:
text.DRV: 17:24 The God that made the world and all things that are in it, he being Lord of Heaven & earth dwelleth not in temples made with hand,
text.NABRE: 17:24 The God who made the world and all that is in it, the Lord of heaven and earth, does not dwell in sanctuaries made by human hands,

text.NRSV: nor is he served by human hands, as though he needed anything, since he himself gives to all mortals life and breath and all things.
text.Rotherham: nor, by human hands, is waited upon, as though in want of anything, himself, giving unto all life and breath and all things;
text.KJV: 17:25 Neither is worshipped with mens hands as though he needed any thing, seeing hee giueth to all, life and breath, and all things,
text.DRV: 17:25 neither is he served with men's hands, needing any thing, whereas himself giveth life unto all, and breathing, and all things:
text.NABRE: 17:25 nor is he served by human hands because he needs anything. Rather it is he who gives to everyone life and breath and everything.

text.NRSV: From one ancestor he made all nations to inhabit the whole earth, and he allotted the times of their existence and the boundaries of the places where they would live,
text.Rotherham: he made also, of one, every nation of men to dwell upon all the face of the earth,—marking out fitting opportunities, and the bounds of their dwelling place,
text.KJV: 17:26 And hath made of one blood all nations of men, for to dwell on all the face of the earth, and hath determined the times before appointed, and the bounds of their habitation:
text.DRV: 17:26 and he made of one, all mankind, to inhabit upon the whole face of the earth, assigning set times, and the limits of their habitation,
text.NABRE: 17:26 He made from one the whole human race to dwell on the entire surface of the earth, and he fixed the ordered seasons and the boundaries of their regions,

text.NRSV: so that they would search for God and perhaps grope for him and find him—though indeed he is not far from each one of us.
text.Rotherham: that they might be seeking God—if, after all, indeed, they might feel after him and find him,—although, in truth, he is already not far from any one of us.
text.KJV: 17:27 That they should seeke the Lord, if haply they might feele after him and finde him, though he be not farre from euery one of vs.
text.DRV: 17:27 for to seek God, if happily they may feel or find him; although he be not far from every one of us:
text.NABRE: 17:27 so that people might seek God, even perhaps grope for him and find him, though indeed he is not far from any one of us.

text.NRSV: For ‘In him we live and move and have our being’; as even some of your own poets have said, ‘For we too are his offspring.’
text.Rotherham: For, in him, we live and move and are: as, even some of your own poets, have said—For, his offspring also, we are.
text.KJV: 17:28 For in him we liue, and mooue, and haue our being, as certaine also of your owne Poets haue said, For we are also his offspring.
text.DRV: 17:28 For in him we live and move and be, as certain also of your own poets said, For of his kind also we are.
text.NABRE: 17:28 For ‘In him we live and move and have our being,’ as even some of your poets have said, ‘For we too are his offspring.’

text.NRSV: Since we are God’s offspring, we ought not to think that the deity is like gold, or silver, or stone, an image formed by the art and imagination of mortals.
text.Rotherham: Being, then, offspring, of God, we ought not to be supposing that, unto gold or silver or stone, graven by art and device of man, the Divine, is like.
text.KJV: 17:29 Forasmuch then as wee are the offspring of God, wee ought not to thinke that the Godhead is like vnto golde, or siluer, or stone grauen by arte, and mans deuice.
text.DRV: 17:29 Being therefore of God's kind, we may not suppose, the Divinity to be like unto gold or silver, or stone, the graving of art and device of man.
text.NABRE: 17:29 Since therefore we are the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the divinity is like an image fashioned from gold, silver, or stone by human art and imagination.

text.NRSV: While God has overlooked the times of human ignorance, now he commands all people everywhere to repent,
text.Rotherham: The times of ignorance, therefore, overlooking, God, as things now are, is charging all men everywhere to repent,
text.KJV: 17:30 And the times of this ignorance God winked at, but now commandeth all men euery where to repent:
text.DRV: 17:30 And the times truly of this ignorance whereas God despised, now he denounceth unto men that all every-where do penance,
text.NABRE: 17:30 God has overlooked the times of ignorance, but now he demands that all people everywhere repent

text.NRSV: because he has fixed a day on which he will have the world judged in righteousness by a man whom he has appointed, and of this he has given assurance to all by raising him from the dead.”
text.Rotherham: inasmuch as he hath appointed a day, in which he is about to be judging the habitable earth in righteousness, by a man whom he hath pointed out,—offering faith unto all, by raising him from among the dead?
text.KJV: 17:31 Because hee hath appointed a day in the which he will iudge the world in righteousnesse, by that man whom hee hath ordeined, whereof he hath giuen assurance vnto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.
text.DRV: 17:31 for that he hath appointed a day wherein he will judge the world in equity, by a man whom he hath appointed, giving all men faith, raising him up from the dead.
text.NABRE: 17:31 because he has established a day on which he will ‘judge the world with justice’ through a man he has appointed, and he has provided confirmation for all by raising him from the dead.”

text.NRSV: When they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some scoffed; but others said, “We will hear you again about this.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard of raising the dead, some, indeed, began to mock, while, others, said—We will hear thee, concerning this, even again.
text.KJV: 17:32 ¶ And when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked: and others said, Wee will heare thee againe of this matter.
text.DRV: 17:32 And when they had heard the resurrection of the dead, certain indeed mocked, but certain said: We will hear thee again concerning this point.
text.NABRE: 17:32 When they heard about resurrection of the dead, some began to scoff, but others said, “We should like to hear you on this some other time.”

text.NRSV: At that point Paul left them.
text.Rotherham: Thus, Paul, came forth out of their midst.
text.KJV: 17:33 So Paul departed from among them.
text.DRV: 17:33 So Paul went forth out of the midst of them.
text.NABRE: 17:33 And so Paul left them.

text.NRSV: But some of them joined him and became believers, including Dionysius the Areopagite and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
text.Rotherham: But, certain persons, joining themselves unto him, believed; among whom were even Dionysius the Mars-hill judge, and a woman by name Damaris, and others with them.
text.KJV: 17:34 Howbeit, certaine men claue vnto him, and beleeued: among the which was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
text.DRV: 17:34 But certain men joining unto him, did believe: among whom was also Dionysius Areopagita, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
text.NABRE: 17:34 But some did join him, and became believers. Among them were Dionysius, a member of the Court of the Areopagus, a woman named Damaris, and others with them.

text.NRSV: After this Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
text.Rotherham: After these things, withdrawing from Athens, he came unto Corinth;
text.KJV: 18:1 After these things, Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth,
text.DRV: 18:1 After these things, departing from Athens, he came to Corinth.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 - Paul in Corinth. After this he left Athens and went to Corinth.

text.NRSV: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul went to see them,
text.Rotherham: and, finding a certain Jew, by name Aquila, of Pontus by birth,—lately come from Italy, and Priscilla his wife, because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to be leaving Rome, he came unto them,
text.KJV: 18:2 And found a certaine Iewe named Aquila, borne in Pontus, lately come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla, (because that Claudius had commanded all Iewes to depart from Rome) and came vnto them.
text.DRV: 18:2 and finding a certain Jew, named Aquila, born in Pontus, who of late was come out of Italy, & Priscilla his wife (because Claudius had commanded all Jews to depart from Rome,) he came to them.
text.NABRE: 18:2 There he met a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to leave Rome. He went to visit them

text.NRSV: and, because he was of the same trade, he stayed with them, and they worked together—by trade they were tentmakers.
text.Rotherham: and, because he was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought, for they were tent-makers by their trade.
text.KJV: 18:3 And because hee was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought (for by their occupation they were tentmakers.)
text.DRV: 18:3 And because he was of the same craft, he remained with them, and wrought, (and they were tent-makers by their craft.)
text.NABRE: 18:3 and, because he practiced the same trade, stayed with them and worked, for they were tentmakers by trade.

text.NRSV: Every sabbath he would argue in the synagogue and would try to convince Jews and Greeks.
text.Rotherham: And he began reasoning in the synagogue every sabbath, and was persuading both Jews and Greeks.
text.KJV: 18:4 And hee reasoned in the Synagogue euery Sabbath, and perswaded the Iewes, and the Greekes.
text.DRV: 18:4 And he disputed in the synagogue every Sabboth, interposing the name of our Lord Jesus, and he exhorted the Jews and the Greeks.
text.NABRE: 18:4 Every sabbath, he entered into discussions in the synagogue, attempting to convince both Jews and Greeks.

text.NRSV: When Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul was occupied with proclaiming the word, testifying to the Jews that the Messiah was Jesus.
text.Rotherham: When, however, both Silas and Timothy had come down from Macedonia, Paul began to be urged on in the word, bearing full witness unto the Jews that, Jesus, was, the Christ.
text.KJV: 18:5 And when Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in spirit, and testified to the Iewes, that Iesus was Christ.
text.DRV: 18:5 And when Silas and Timothee were come from Macedonia, Paul was instant in preaching, testifying to the Jews that Jesus is Christ.
text.NABRE: 18:5 When Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia, Paul began to occupy himself totally with preaching the word, testifying to the Jews that the Messiah was Jesus.

text.NRSV: When they opposed and reviled him, in protest he shook the dust from his clothes and said to them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am innocent. From now on I will go to the Gentiles.”
text.Rotherham: But, as they began opposing and defaming, shaking out his garments, he said unto them—Your blood, be upon your own head! Pure, am, I: henceforth, unto the nations, will I go.
text.KJV: 18:6 And when they opposed themselues, and blasphemed, he shooke his raiment, and said vnto them, Your blood be vpon your owne heads, I am cleane: from henceforth I will goe vnto the Gentiles.
text.DRV: 18:6 But they contradicting and blaspheming, he shaking his garments, said to them: Your blood upon your own head: I being clean, from henceforth will go to the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: 18:6 When they opposed him and reviled him, he shook out his garments and said to them, “Your blood be on your heads! I am clear of responsibility. From now on I will go to the Gentiles.”

text.NRSV: Then he left the synagogue and went to the house of a man named Titius Justus, a worshiper of God; his house was next door to the synagogue.
text.Rotherham: And, removing from thence, he came into the house of a certain man by name Titius Justus, who worshipped God, whose house was adjoining unto the synagogue.
text.KJV: 18:7 ¶ And hee departed thence, and entred into a certaine mans house, named Iustus, one that worshipped God, whose house ioyned hard to the Synagogue.
text.DRV: 18:7 And departing thence, he entered into the house of a certain man, named Titus Justus, one that served God, whose house was adjoining to the synagogue.
text.NABRE: 18:7 So he left there and went to a house belonging to a man named Titus Justus, a worshiper of God; his house was next to a synagogue.

text.NRSV: Crispus, the official of the synagogue, became a believer in the Lord, together with all his household; and many of the Corinthians who heard Paul became believers and were baptized.
text.Rotherham: But, Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord, with all his house. And, many of the Corinthians, hearing, were believing, and being immersed.
text.KJV: 18:8 And Crispus, the chiefe ruler of the Synagogue, beleeued on the Lord, with all his house: and many of the Corinthians, hearing, beleeued, and were baptized.
text.DRV: 18:8 And Crispus the prince of the Synagogue believed our Lord, with all his house: & many of the Corinthians hearing believed, and were baptized.
text.NABRE: 18:8 Crispus, the synagogue official, came to believe in the Lord along with his entire household, and many of the Corinthians who heard believed and were baptized.

text.NRSV: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, “Do not be afraid, but speak and do not be silent;
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said by night, through means of a vision, unto Paul—Be not afraid! but be speaking,—and do not hold thy peace;
text.KJV: 18:9 Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, Be not afraid, but speake, and holde not thy peace:
text.DRV: 18:9 And our Lord said in the night by a vision to Paul: Do not fear, but speak, and hold not thy peace,
text.NABRE: 18:9 One night in a vision the Lord said to Paul, “Do not be afraid. Go on speaking, and do not be silent,

text.NRSV: for I am with you, and no one will lay a hand on you to harm you, for there are many in this city who are my people.”
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, I, am with thee, and, no one, shall set upon thee to harm thee; inasmuch as I have much people in this city.
text.KJV: 18:10 For I am with thee, and no man shal set on thee, to hurt thee: for I haue much people in this city.
text.DRV: 18:10 for-because I am with thee: and no man shall set upon thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.
text.NABRE: 18:10 for I am with you. No one will attack and harm you, for I have many people in this city.”

text.NRSV: He stayed there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.
text.Rotherham: And he remained a year and six months, teaching among them the word of God.
text.KJV: 18:11 And hee continued there a yeere and sixe monethes, teaching the word of God among them.
text.DRV: 18:11 And he sat there a year & six months, teaching among them the word of God.
text.NABRE: 18:11 He settled there for a year and a half and taught the word of God among them.

text.NRSV: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews made a united attack on Paul and brought him before the tribunal.
text.Rotherham: But, when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews, with one accord, set upon Paul, and led him unto the judgment-seat,
text.KJV: 18:12 ¶ And when Gallio was the Deputie of Achaia, the Iewes made insurrection with one accord against Paul, and brought him to the iudgement seat,
text.DRV: 18:12 But Gallio being Proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul, and brought him to the judgement seat,
text.NABRE: 18:12 Accusations Before Gallio. But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews rose up together against Paul and brought him to the tribunal,

text.NRSV: They said, “This man is persuading people to worship God in ways that are contrary to the law.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Contrary to the law, is this one seducing men to be worshipping God.
text.KJV: 18:13 Saying, This fellow perswadeth men to worship God contrary to the Law.
text.DRV: 18:13 saying: That this man contrary to the Law persuadeth men to worship God.
text.NABRE: 18:13 saying, “This man is inducing people to worship God contrary to the law.”

text.NRSV: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, “If it were a matter of misfortune or a villainous crime, I would be justified in accepting the complaint of you Jews;
text.Rotherham: But, as Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews—If, indeed, it had been some wrong or wicked recklessness, O Jews, with reason, in that case, should I have been bearing with you.
text.KJV: 18:14 And when Paul was now about to open his mouth, Gallio said vnto the Iewes, If it were a matter of wrong, or wicked lewdnesse, O yee Iewes, reason would that I should beare with you.
text.DRV: 18:14 And Paul beginning to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews: If it were some unjust thing, or an heinous fact, O you men Jews, I should by reason bear you.
text.NABRE: 18:14 When Paul was about to reply, Gallio spoke to the Jews, “If it were a matter of some crime or malicious fraud, I should with reason hear the complaint of you Jews;

text.NRSV: but since it is a matter of questions about words and names and your own law, see to it yourselves; I do not wish to be a judge of these matters.”
text.Rotherham: If, however, they are questions concerning discourse, and names, and law, that which ye have, ye shall see to it, yourselves; A judge of these things, I, am not disposed to be.
text.KJV: 18:15 But if it be a question of words, and names, and of your law, looke ye to it: for I wil be no iudge of such matters.
text.DRV: 18:15 But if they be questions of word and names, and of your law, yourselves look unto it: I will not be judge of these things.
text.NABRE: 18:15 but since it is a question of arguments over doctrine and titles and your own law, see to it yourselves. I do not wish to be a judge of such matters.”

text.NRSV: And he dismissed them from the tribunal.
text.Rotherham: And he drove them from the judgment-seat.
text.KJV: 18:16 And he draue them from the iudgment seate.
text.DRV: 18:16 And he drove them from the judgement seat.
text.NABRE: 18:16 And he drove them away from the tribunal.

text.NRSV: Then all of them seized Sosthenes, the official of the synagogue, and beat him in front of the tribunal. But Gallio paid no attention to any of these things.
text.Rotherham: But they all, laying hold of Sosthenes the ruler of the synagogue, began to strike him before the judgment-seat; and, for none of these things, did Gallio care.
text.KJV: 18:17 Then all the Greekes tooke Sosthenes the chiefe ruler of the Synagogue, and beat him before the Iudgement seat: and Gallio cared for none of those things.
text.DRV: 18:17 And all apprehending Sosthenes the prince of the Synagogue, strook him before the judgement seat: and Gallio cared for none of those things.
text.NABRE: 18:17 They all seized Sosthenes, the synagogue official, and beat him in full view of the tribunal. But none of this was of concern to Gallio.

text.NRSV: After staying there for a considerable time, Paul said farewell to the believers and sailed for Syria, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila. At Cenchreae he had his hair cut, for he was under a vow.
text.Rotherham: Paul, however, still further abiding a good many days with the brethren, bidding them adieu, set sail for Syria; and, with him, Priscilla and Aquila; having shorn his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow.
text.KJV: 18:18 ¶ And Paul after this taried there yet a good while, and then tooke his leaue of the brethren, and sailed thence into Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila: hauing shorne his head in Cenchrea: for he had a vow.
text.DRV: 18:18 But Paul when he had stayed yet many days, taking his leave of the brethren, sailed to Syria, (and with him Priscilla and Aquila,) who had shorn his head in Cenchris. For he had a vow.
text.NABRE: 18:18 Return to Syrian Antioch. Paul remained for quite some time, and after saying farewell to the brothers he sailed for Syria, together with Priscilla and Aquila. At Cenchreae he had his hair cut because he had taken a vow.

text.NRSV: When they reached Ephesus, he left them there, but first he himself went into the synagogue and had a discussion with the Jews.
text.Rotherham: And they came down to Ephesus; and, as for them, he left them there,—but, himself entering into the synagogue, he reasoned with the Jews.
text.KJV: 18:19 And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himselfe entred into the Synagogue, and reasoned with the Iewes.
text.DRV: 18:19 And he came unto Ephesus, and them he left there. But himself entering into the Synagogue, disputed with the Jews.
text.NABRE: 18:19 When they reached Ephesus, he left them there, while he entered the synagogue and held discussions with the Jews.

text.NRSV: When they asked him to stay longer, he declined;
text.Rotherham: And, when they requested him for a longer time to abide, he consented not;
text.KJV: 18:20 When they desired him to tary longer time with them, hee consented not:
text.DRV: 18:20 And when they desired him, that he would tarry a longer time, he consented not,
text.NABRE: 18:20 Although they asked him to stay for a longer time, he did not consent,

text.NRSV: but on taking leave of them, he said, “I will return to you, if God wills.” Then he set sail from Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: but, bidding them adieu, and saying—Again, will I return unto you, God willing, he sailed away from Ephesus;
text.KJV: 18:21 But bade them farewell, saying, I must by all meanes keepe this feast that commeth, in Hierusalem; but I will returne againe vnto you, if God will: and he sailed from Ephesus.
text.DRV: 18:21 but taking his leave, and saying: I will return to you again God willing, he departed from Ephesus.
text.NABRE: 18:21 but as he said farewell he promised, “I shall come back to you again, God willing.” Then he set sail from Ephesus.

text.NRSV: When he had landed at Caesarea, he went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: and, putting in at Caesarea, going up and saluting the assembly, went down unto Antioch;
text.KJV: 18:22 And when he had landed at Cesarea, and gone vp, and saluted the Church, he went downe to Antioch.
text.DRV: 18:22 And going down to Cæsarea, he went up, and saluted the Church, and came down to Antioch.
text.NABRE: 18:22 Upon landing at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the church and then went down to Antioch.

text.NRSV: After spending some time there he departed and went from place to place through the region of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
text.Rotherham: and, spending some time, he went forth, passing through, in order, the country of Galatia and Phrygia, confirming all the disciples.
text.KJV: 18:23 And after he had spent some time there, hee departed, and went ouer all the countrey of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.
text.DRV: 18:23 And having tarried there a certain time, he departed, walking in order through the country of Galatia and Phrygia, confirming all the Disciples.
text.NABRE: 18:23 After staying there some time, he left and traveled in orderly sequence through the Galatian country and Phrygia, bringing strength to all the disciples.

text.NRSV: Now there came to Ephesus a Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria. He was an eloquent man, well-versed in the scriptures.
text.Rotherham: But, a certain Jew, Apollos by name, an Alexandrian by birth, a learned man, came down to Ephesus, being, mighty, in the Scriptures.
text.KJV: 18:24 ¶ And a certaine Iew, named Apollos, borne at Alexandria, an eloquent man, and mightie in the Scriptures, came to Ephesus.
text.DRV: 18:24 And a certain Jew, named Apollo, born at Alexandria, an eloquent man, came to Ephesus, mighty in the Scriptures.
text.NABRE: 18:24 Apollos. A Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria, an eloquent speaker, arrived in Ephesus. He was an authority on the scriptures.

text.NRSV: He had been instructed in the Way of the Lord; and he spoke with burning enthusiasm and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, though he knew only the baptism of John.
text.Rotherham: The same, had been orally taught the way of the Lord, and, being fervent in his spirit, began speaking and teaching accurately, the things concerning Jesus,—properly knowing, only the immersion of John.
text.KJV: 18:25 This man was instructed in the way of the Lord, and being feruent in the spirit, he spake and taught diligently the things of the Lord, knowing onely the baptisme of Iohn.
text.DRV: 18:25 This man was taught the way of our Lord: and being fervent in spirit he spake, & taught diligently those things that pertain to Jesus, knowing only the Baptism of John.
text.NABRE: 18:25 He had been instructed in the Way of the Lord and, with ardent spirit, spoke and taught accurately about Jesus, although he knew only the baptism of John.

text.NRSV: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue; but when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside and explained the Way of God to him more accurately.
text.Rotherham: The same, also began speaking boldly in the synagogue; and Priscilla and Aquila, hearing him, took him unto them, and, more accurately, expounded unto him the way of God.
text.KJV: 18:26 And he began to speake boldly in the Synagogue: whom when Aquila and Priscilla had heard, they tooke him vnto them, and expounded vnto him the way of God more perfectly.
text.DRV: 18:26 This man therefore began to deal confidently in the Synagogue. Whom when Priscilla and Aquila had heard, they took him unto them, and expounded to him the way of our Lord more diligently.
text.NABRE: 18:26 He began to speak boldly in the synagogue; but when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside and explained to him the Way [of God] more accurately.

text.NRSV: And when he wished to cross over to Achaia, the believers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples to welcome him. On his arrival he greatly helped those who through grace had become believers,
text.Rotherham: And, he being minded to pass through into Achaia, the brethren urgently wrote unto the disciples to welcome him,—who, arriving, was very useful unto them who had believed, with his gift;
text.KJV: 18:27 And when hee was disposed to passe into Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receiue him: who, when he was come, helped them much which had beleeued throgh grace.
text.DRV: 18:27 And whereas he was desirous to go to Achaia, the brethren exhorting wrote to the Disciples to receive him. Who, when he was come, profited them much that had believed.
text.NABRE: 18:27 And when he wanted to cross to Achaia, the brothers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples there to welcome him. After his arrival he gave great assistance to those who had come to believe through grace.

text.NRSV: for he powerfully refuted the Jews in public, showing by the scriptures that the Messiah is Jesus.
text.Rotherham: for, with great force, began he confuting the Jews, publicly, shewing by the Scriptures that Jesus was, the Christ.
text.KJV: 18:28 For hee mightily conuinced the Iewes, and that publikely, shewing by the scriptures, that Iesus was Christ.
text.DRV: 18:28 For he with vehemency convinced the Jews openly, shewing by the scriptures, that Jesus is Christ.
text.NABRE: 18:28 He vigorously refuted the Jews in public, establishing from the scriptures that the Messiah is Jesus.

text.NRSV: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul passed through the interior regions and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while Apollos was in Corinth, Paul, passing through the upper parts, came to Ephesus, and found certain disciples;
text.KJV: 19:1 And it came to passe, that while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul hauing passed thorow the vpper coasts, came to Ephesus, and finding certaine disciples,
text.DRV: 19:1 And it came to pass when Apollo was at Corinth, that Paul having gone through the higher parts came to Ephesus, and found certain Disciples:
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Paul in Ephesus. While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled through the interior of the country and came [down] to Ephesus where he found some disciples.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you became believers?” They replied, “No, we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto them—Holy Spirit, received ye, when ye believed? And they said unto him—Nay! not even whether there is Holy Spirit, did we hear.
text.KJV: 19:2 He said vnto them, Haue ye receiued the holy Ghost since yee beleeued? And they saide vnto him, Wee haue not so much as heard whether there be any holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 19:2 and he said to them: Have you received the Holy Ghost, believing? But they said to him: Nay, neither have we heard whether there be a Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 19:2 He said to them, “Did you receive the holy Spirit when you became believers?” They answered him, “We have never even heard that there is a holy Spirit.”

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Into what then were you baptized?” They answered, “Into John’s baptism.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Into what, then, were ye immersed? And, they, said—Unto John’s immersion.
text.KJV: 19:3 And he said vnto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they saide, Unto Iohns Baptisme.
text.DRV: 19:3 But he said: In what then were you baptized? Who said, In John's Baptism.
text.NABRE: 19:3 He said, “How were you baptized?” They replied, “With the baptism of John.”

text.NRSV: Paul said, “John baptized with the baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Paul—John, immersed with an immersion of repentance, unto the people, saying, that, on him who was coming after him, they should believe,—that is, on Jesus.
text.KJV: 19:4 Then saide Paul, Iohn verely baptized with the baptisme of repentance, saying vnto the people, that they should beleeue on him which should come after him, that is, on Christ Iesus.
text.DRV: 19:4 And Paul said: John baptized the people with the Baptism of penance, saying, That they should believe in him that was to come after him, that is to say, in Jesus.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Paul then said, “John baptized with a baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.”

text.NRSV: On hearing this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, when they heard [this], they were immersed into the name of the Lord Jesus;
text.KJV: 19:5 When they heard this, they were baptized in the Name of the Lord Iesus.
text.DRV: 19:5 Hearing these things, they were baptized in the name of our Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: 19:5 When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

text.NRSV: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied—
text.Rotherham: and, Paul laying hands upon them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they began speaking with tongues and prophesying.
text.KJV: 19:6 And when Paul had laide his hands vpon them, the holy Ghost came on them, and they spake with tongues, and prophecied.
text.DRV: 19:6 And when Paul had imposed hands on them, the Holy Ghost came upon them, & they spake with tongues, and prophesied.
text.NABRE: 19:6 And when Paul laid [his] hands on them, the holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied.

text.NRSV: altogether there were about twelve of them.
text.Rotherham: And all the men were about twelve.
text.KJV: 19:7 And all þ men were about twelue.
text.DRV: 19:7 And all the men were about twelve.
text.NABRE: 19:7 Altogether there were about twelve men.

text.NRSV: He entered the synagogue and for three months spoke out boldly, and argued persuasively about the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into the synagogue, he was speaking boldly for three months, reasoning and persuading concerning the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: 19:8 And hee went into the Synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three moneths, disputing and perswading the things concerning the Kingdome of God.
text.DRV: 19:8 And entering into the Synagogue, he spake confidently for three months, disputing & exhorting of the Kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: 19:8 He entered the synagogue, and for three months debated boldly with persuasive arguments about the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: When some stubbornly refused to believe and spoke evil of the Way before the congregation, he left them, taking the disciples with him, and argued daily in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.
text.Rotherham: But, when some were hardening themselves, and refusing to be persuaded, speaking evil of the Way before the throng, withdrawing from them, he separated the disciples; day by day, reasoning in the school of Tyrannus.
text.KJV: 19:9 But when diuers were hardened, and beleeued not, but spake euill of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the schoole of one Tyrannus.
text.DRV: 19:9 But when certain were indurate, and believed not, ill-speaking the way of our Lord before the multitude, departing from them, he separated the Disciples, daily disputing in the school of one Tyrannus.
text.NABRE: 19:9 But when some in their obstinacy and disbelief disparaged the Way before the assembly, he withdrew and took his disciples with him and began to hold daily discussions in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.

text.NRSV: This continued for two years, so that all the residents of Asia, both Jews and Greeks, heard the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, this, took place for two years, so that, all who dwelt in Asia, heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.
text.KJV: 19:10 And this continued by the space of two yeeres, so that all they which dwelt in Asia, heard the word of the Lord Iesus, both Iewes and Greeks.
text.DRV: 19:10 And this was done for the space of two years, so that all which dwelt in Asia, heard the word of our Lord, Jews and Gentiles.
text.NABRE: 19:10 This continued for two years with the result that all the inhabitants of the province of Asia heard the word of the Lord, Jews and Greeks alike.

text.NRSV: God did extraordinary miracles through Paul,
text.Rotherham: Mighty works, also, not the ordinary, God was working through the hands of Paul;
text.KJV: 19:11 And God wrought speciall miracles by the hands of Paul:
text.DRV: 19:11 And God wrought by the hand of Paul miracles not common:
text.NABRE: 19:11 So extraordinary were the mighty deeds God accomplished at the hands of Paul

text.NRSV: so that when the handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his skin were brought to the sick, their diseases left them, and the evil spirits came out of them.
text.Rotherham: so that, even unto the sick, were being carried from his body, handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases were departing from them, and, the wicked spirits, were going out.
text.KJV: 19:12 So that from his body were brought vnto the sicke handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases departed from them, and the euill spirits went out of them.
text.DRV: 19:12 so that there were also brought from his body napkins or handkerchiefs upon the sick, and the diseases departed from them, and the wicked spirits went out.
text.NABRE: 19:12 that when face cloths or aprons that touched his skin were applied to the sick, their diseases left them and the evil spirits came out of them.

text.NRSV: Then some itinerant Jewish exorcists tried to use the name of the Lord Jesus over those who had evil spirits, saying, “I adjure you by the Jesus whom Paul proclaims.”
text.Rotherham: But certain also of the wandering Jews, exorcists, took in hand to be naming, over them that had the wicked spirits, the name of the Lord Jesus, saying—I adjure you, by Jesus whom Paul proclaimeth!
text.KJV: 19:13 ¶ Then certaine of the vagabond Iewes, exorcistes, tooke vpon them to call ouer them which had euill spirits, the Name of the Lord Iesus, saying, We adiure you by Iesus whom Paul preacheth.
text.DRV: 19:13 And certain also of the Judaical exorcists that went about, assayed to invocate upon them that had evil spirits, the name of our Lord Jesus, saying: I adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preacheth.
text.NABRE: 19:13 The Jewish Exorcists. Then some itinerant Jewish exorcists tried to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those with evil spirits, saying, “I adjure you by the Jesus whom Paul preaches.”

text.NRSV: Seven sons of a Jewish high priest named Sceva were doing this.
text.Rotherham: And there were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jew, a High-priest, who, this thing, were doing.
text.KJV: 19:14 And there were seuen sonnes of one Sceua a Iewe, and chiefe of the Priests, which did so.
text.DRV: 19:14 And there were certain sons of Sceva a Jew, chief Priest, seven, that did this.
text.NABRE: 19:14 When the seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish high priest, tried to do this,

text.NRSV: But the evil spirit answered them, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are you?”
text.Rotherham: But the wicked spirit. Answering, said unto them—Jesus, [indeed], I am getting to know, and, Paul, I well-know,—but who are, ye?
text.KJV: 19:15 And the euill spirit answered, and said, Iesus I knowe, and Paul I know, but who are ye?
text.DRV: 19:15 But the wicked spirit answering, said to them: Jesus I know, and Paul I know: but you, what are ye?
text.NABRE: 19:15 the evil spirit said to them in reply, “Jesus I recognize, Paul I know, but who are you?”

text.NRSV: Then the man with the evil spirit leaped on them, mastered them all, and so overpowered them that they fled out of the house naked and wounded.
text.Rotherham: And the man in whom was the wicked spirit, springing upon them, mastering them both, prevailed against them, so that, naked and wounded, fled they out of that house.
text.KJV: 19:16 And the man in whom the euill spirit was, leapt on them, and ouercame them, and preuailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
text.DRV: 19:16 And the man in whom the wicked spirit was, leaping upon them, and mastering both, prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
text.NABRE: 19:16 The person with the evil spirit then sprang at them and subdued them all. He so overpowered them that they fled naked and wounded from that house.

text.NRSV: When this became known to all residents of Ephesus, both Jews and Greeks, everyone was awestruck; and the name of the Lord Jesus was praised.
text.Rotherham: And, this, became known to all—both Jews and Greeks—who were dwelling in Ephesus; and fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was being magnified.
text.KJV: 19:17 And this was knowen to all the Iewes and Greekes also dwelling at Ephesus, and feare fell on them all, and the Name of the Lord Iesus was magnified.
text.DRV: 19:17 And this was made notorious to all the Jews and the Gentiles that dwelt at Ephesus: and fear fell upon all them, and the name of our Lord Jesus was magnified.
text.NABRE: 19:17 When this became known to all the Jews and Greeks who lived in Ephesus, fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was held in great esteem.

text.NRSV: Also many of those who became believers confessed and disclosed their practices.
text.Rotherham: Many also of them who had believed, were coming, making open confession, and renouncing their practices.
text.KJV: 19:18 And many that beleeued came, and confessed, and shewed their deedes.
text.DRV: 19:18 And many of them that believed, came confessing & declaring their deeds.
text.NABRE: 19:18 Many of those who had become believers came forward and openly acknowledged their former practices.

text.NRSV: A number of those who practiced magic collected their books and burned them publicly; when the value of these was calculated, it was found to come to fifty thousand silver coins.
text.Rotherham: And, a good many of them who had practised the curious arts, bringing together the books, were burning them before all; and they reckoned up the prices of them, and found them fifty thousand pieces of silver.
text.KJV: 19:19 Many also of them which vsed curious arts, brought their bookes together and burned them before all men: and they counted the price of them, and found it fifty thousand pieces of siluer.
text.DRV: 19:19 And many of them that had followed curious things, brought together their books, and burnt them before all: and counting the prices of them, they found the money to be fifty thousand pence.
text.NABRE: 19:19 Moreover, a large number of those who had practiced magic collected their books and burned them in public. They calculated their value and found it to be fifty thousand silver pieces.

text.NRSV: So the word of the Lord grew mightily and prevailed.
text.Rotherham: Thus, with might, the Lord’s word, was growing and prevailing.
text.KJV: 19:20 So mightily grew the word of God, and preuailed.
text.DRV: 19:20 So mightily increased the word of God and was confirmed.
text.NABRE: 19:20 Thus did the word of the Lord continue to spread with influence and power.

text.NRSV: Now after these things had been accomplished, Paul resolved in the Spirit to go through Macedonia and Achaia, and then to go on to Jerusalem. He said, “After I have gone there, I must also see Rome.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when these things were fulfilled, Paul purposed in his spirit, going through Macedonia and Achaia, to be journeying unto Jerusalem, saying—After I have been there, Rome also, must I see!
text.KJV: 19:21 ¶ After these things were ended, Paul purposed in the spirit, when hee had passed thorow Macedonia and Athaia, to go to Hierusalem, saying, After I haue bin there, I must also see Rome.
text.DRV: 19:21 And when these things were ended, Paul purposed in the Spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying: After I shall have been there, I must see Rome also.
text.NABRE: 19:21 Paul’s Plans. When this was concluded, Paul made up his mind to travel through Macedonia and Achaia, and then to go on to Jerusalem, saying, “After I have been there, I must visit Rome also.”

text.NRSV: So he sent two of his helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he himself stayed for some time in Asia.
text.Rotherham: And, sending off into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself, held on awhile in Asia.
text.KJV: 19:22 So hee sent into Macedonia two of them that ministred vnto him, Timotheus and Erastus, but he himselfe stayed in Asia for a season.
text.DRV: 19:22 And sending into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timothee and Erastus, himself remained for a time in Asia.
text.NABRE: 19:22 Then he sent to Macedonia two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, while he himself stayed for a while in the province of Asia.

text.NRSV: About that time no little disturbance arose concerning the Way.
text.Rotherham: And there arose during that season no small disturbance concerning the Way.
text.KJV: 19:23 And the same time there arose no small stirre about that way.
text.DRV: 19:23 And at that time there was made no little trouble about the way of our Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:23 The Riot of the Silversmiths. About that time a serious disturbance broke out concerning the Way.

text.NRSV: A man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the artisans.
text.Rotherham: For, one Demetrius by name, a silversmith, making [silver] shrines, of Diana, used to bring unto the craftsmen no little business;
text.KJV: 19:24 For a certaine man named Demetrius, a siluer smith, which made siluer shrines for Diana, brought no small gaine vnto the craftsmen:
text.DRV: 19:24 For one named Demetrius, a silver-smith, that made silver temples of Diana, procured to the artificers no small gain:
text.NABRE: 19:24 There was a silversmith named Demetrius who made miniature silver shrines of Artemis and provided no little work for the craftsmen.

text.NRSV: These he gathered together, with the workers of the same trade, and said, “Men, you know that we get our wealth from this business.
text.Rotherham: gathering whom together, and them who in such things wrought, he said—Men! ye well know that, by this business, we have our prosperity;
text.KJV: 19:25 Whom he called together, with the workemen of like occupation, and said, Sirs, ye know that by this craft we haue our wealth.
text.DRV: 19:25 whom calling together and them that were the same kind of workmen, he said: Sirs, you know that our gain is of this occupation;
text.NABRE: 19:25 He called a meeting of these and other workers in related crafts and said, “Men, you know well that our prosperity derives from this work.

text.NRSV: You also see and hear that not only in Ephesus but in almost the whole of Asia this Paul has persuaded and drawn away a considerable number of people by saying that gods made with hands are not gods.
text.Rotherham: and ye perceive and hear that, not only in Ephesus, but well-nigh in all Asia, this Paul, hath persuaded and turned away a considerable multitude, saying that they are, no gods, which, with hands, are made.
text.KJV: 19:26 Moreouer, ye see & heare, that not alone at Ephesus, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul hath perswaded and turned away much people, saying, that they bee no gods, which are made with hands.
text.DRV: 19:26 and you see, and hear that this same Paul by persuasion hath averted a great multitude not only of Ephesus, but almost of all Asia, saying: That they are not gods which be made by hands.
text.NABRE: 19:26 As you can now see and hear, not only in Ephesus but throughout most of the province of Asia this Paul has persuaded and misled a great number of people by saying that gods made by hands are not gods at all.

text.NRSV: And there is danger not only that this trade of ours may come into disrepute but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be scorned, and she will be deprived of her majesty that brought all Asia and the world to worship her.”
text.Rotherham: And, not only is there danger that this our heritage into ill-repute may come, but even that the temple, of the great Goddess Diana, for nothing, may be counted; also that, even on the point of being pulled down, may be Her Majesty, whom, all Asia and the habitable world, do worship.
text.KJV: 19:27 So that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought: but also that the Temple of the great goddesse Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia, and the world worshippeth.
text.DRV: 19:27 And not only unto us is this part in danger to be reproved, but also the temple of great Diana shall be reputed for nothing, yea & her majesty shall begin to be destroyed, whom all Asia & the world worshippeth.
text.NABRE: 19:27 The danger grows, not only that our business will be discredited, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be of no account, and that she whom the whole province of Asia and all the world worship will be stripped of her magnificence.”

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were enraged and shouted, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
text.Rotherham: Now, hearing this, and becoming full of wrath, they began crying aloud, saying—Great, is Diana of the Ephesians!
text.KJV: 19:28 And when they heard these sayings, they were ful of wrath, & cried out, saying, Great is Diana of þ Ephesians.
text.DRV: 19:28 Hearing these things they were replenished with anger, and cried out saying: Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
text.NABRE: 19:28 When they heard this, they were filled with fury and began to shout, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

text.NRSV: The city was filled with the confusion; and they rushed together to the theater, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians who were Paul’s companions in travel.
text.Rotherham: And the city was filled with the confusion; they rushed also with one accord into the theatre, carrying off with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians, fellow-travellers of Paul.
text.KJV: 19:29 And the whole citie was filled with confusion, and hauing caught Gaius and Aristarchus men of Macedonia Pauls companions in trauaile, they rushed with one accord into the Theatre.
text.DRV: 19:29 And the whole city was filled with confusion, and they ran violently with one accord into the theater, catching Gaius & Aristarchus Macedonians, Paul's companions.
text.NABRE: 19:29 The city was filled with confusion, and the people rushed with one accord into the theater, seizing Gaius and Aristarchus, the Macedonians, Paul’s traveling companions.

text.NRSV: Paul wished to go into the crowd, but the disciples would not let him;
text.Rotherham: But, Paul, being minded to enter in among the populace, the disciples would not suffer him.
text.KJV: 19:30 And when Paul would haue entred in vnto the people, the disciples suffered him not.
text.DRV: 19:30 And when Paul would have entered in to the people, the Disciples did not permit him.
text.NABRE: 19:30 Paul wanted to go before the crowd, but the disciples would not let him,

text.NRSV: even some officials of the province of Asia, who were friends of his, sent him a message urging him not to venture into the theater.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, certain of the Asiarchs also, being his friends, sending unto him, were beseeching him not to adventure himself into the theatre.
text.KJV: 19:31 And certaine of the chiefe of Asia, which were his friends, sent vnto him, desiring him that he would not aduenture himselfe into the Theatre.
text.DRV: 19:31 And certain also of the Princes of Asia that were his friends, sent unto him, desiring that he would not adventure himself into the theater:
text.NABRE: 19:31 and even some of the Asiarchs who were friends of his sent word to him advising him not to venture into the theater.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile, some were shouting one thing, some another; for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not know why they had come together.
text.Rotherham: Others, indeed, were crying out something else; for the assembly had become confused, and, the greater part, knew not for what cause they had come together.
text.KJV: 19:32 Some therefore cried one thing, and some another: for the assembly was confused, and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together.
text.DRV: 19:32 and others cried another thing. For the assembly was confuse, & the more part knew not for what cause they were assembled.
